{{otheruses|Shinto shrine in Kyoto, Japan|Shinto shrine in Hiroshima, Japan|Okazaki Shrine (Hiroshima)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |Name = Okazaki Shrine |Image = [[File:Okazaki-jinja haiden.jpg|260px]]
Haiden |Location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Sakyo Ward]], [[Okazaki (Kyoto)|Okazaki]] Higashitenno-cho 51
{{Cite web|url=http://www.okazakijinja.jp/access/|title=Access|publisher=Okazaki Shrine|accessdate=2017-01-25|ref=}} |Coordinates={{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point|frame-width=200}} |Deity = [[Susanoo|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]]
[[Gonansanjoshin|Three Female and Five Male Deities]] |Shintai = |Shrine rank = Former [[Gōsha|District Shrine]] |Established = [[Enryaku]] 13 (794 AD) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Other names = Higashitenno-sha, Higashitenno |Pilgrimage = [[Kyoto Jurokushajin]]
[[Zenkoku Seiseikai]] (59 Shrines Enshrining Susanoo-no-Mikoto) |Annual festival = [[July 16]] (Summer Purification Festival) |Rituals = |Map = {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point|frame-width=200}} }} {{osm box|w|1155362848}} '''Okazaki Shrine''' (岡崎神社, Okazaki Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Okazaki (Kyoto)|Okazaki Higashitenno-cho]], [[Sakyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]]. It is also known as "Higashitenno." Since the surrounding area was once inhabited by [[wild rabbits]],{{Cite news|title=Full of rabbits – overcoming hardships with leaps and bounds |newspaper=Asahi Shimbun |page=31 |date=2023-01-16 |author=}} rabbits are considered the shrine’s [[Ujigami|guardian spirits]] and [[shinshi|divine messengers]]. == Overview== Besides being known as a deity of safe childbirth, the shrine is also believed to grant fertility, as rabbits are known for their prolific reproduction. Additionally, its enshrined deities, [[Susanoo|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Kushinadahime]], are associated with having many children. The shrine is also regarded as a place of [[katatagae|directional protection]] due to its historical origins. Several rabbit stone statues can be found throughout the precincts. == History== In [[Enryaku]] 13 (794 AD), during Emperor [[Kanmu]]’s relocation of the capital to [[Heian-kyō]], the shrine was established as one of the [[Taishogun-sha]] (Great General Shrines) built at the four corners of the city to protect the capital. Since it was located to the east, it became known as "Higashitenno."{{Cite book|author= |title=Kyoto Encyclopedia |publisher=Naoya Kaji |date=1984-11-12 |page=135 |isbn=4-473-00885-1}} In [[Jogan]] 11 (869 AD), Emperor [[Seiwa]] ordered the construction of the shrine, enshrining [[Gozu Tenno]] (identified with [[Susanoo]]) from [[Hiromine Shrine]] in [[Harima Province]]. At the time, it served as the guardian shrine of [[Tokoji Temple]], which was located in the same area. In [[Jisho]] 2 (1178 AD), Emperor [[Takakura]] dedicated offerings for the safe childbirth of his co]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category:t, [[Taira no Tokuko]]. In [[Gen'ō]] 1 (1319 AD), Emperor [[Go-Daigo]] rebuilt the shrine and granted it the rank of Shoichii (Senior First Rank) along with sacred treasures. During the [[Ōnin War]], the temple was destroyed by fire, leaving only the shrine intact.{{Cite book|title=Showa Kyoto Guidebook |publisher=Obuchi Kaoru |date=1981-10-30 |page=179}} During the [[Keichō era]] (1596–1615), the shrine was renamed to its current name. In the [[Meiji era]], it was designated as a [[Gōsha|District Shrine]]. On [[September 21, 1934]], the [[Muroto Typhoon]] caused strong winds that collapsed the Haiden (worship hall).Many were trapped under collapsed school buildings in Kyoto. ''Osaka Mainichi Shimbun'' (September 21, 1934). ''Showa News Encyclopedia, Vol. 4 (Showa 8–9)''. == Annual Festivals== A signboard at the entrance lists the annual festivals as follows: * [[January 1]] - New Year's Festival (Saitan-sai) * [[January 16]] - Miyashige Inari Festival / Senbo Festival * [[February 3]] - Setsubun Festival * [[March 16]] - Kinensai (Prayer Festival) * [[March]] (Spring Equinox) - Spring Ancestral Festival * [[May 16]] - Senbo Festival * [[June 30]] - Nagoshi-no-Harae (Summer Purification) * [[July 16]] - Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * [[September 16]] - Senbo Festival * [[September]] (Autumn Equinox) - Autumn Ancestral Festival * [[October 16]] - Parishioners' Festival * Includes a children's procession, children's portable shrine (mikoshi), and hot-water purification ritual (Yugama). Also known as the Okazaki Tenno Festival, Okazaki Festival, or Grand Festival. Historically, there were more than ten large decorative floats (hoko), but they no longer participate in the festival.{{Cite book|author=Takashi Sawa, et al. |title=Kyoto Encyclopedia |publisher=Tankosha |year=1984 |page=135 |isbn=4-473-00885-1}}{{Cite book|author=Itsuo Munemasa, Katsuhisa Moriya |title=Kyoto Saijiki |publisher=Tankosha |year=1986 |page=262 |isbn=4-473-00940-8}} * [[November 16]] - Fire Ritual Festival (Hitaki-sai) * [[November 23]] - Niiname-sai (Harvest Festival) * [[December 2]] - Ame-no-Yashiro Festival * [[December 31]] - Toshikoshi-no-Harae (Year-End Purification) == Enshrined Deities== * Main Deities - [[Susanoo|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Kushinadahime]], [[Gonansanjoshin|Three Female and Five Male Deities]] == Shrine Grounds== * Main Hall * Worship Hall * Ame-no-Yashiro - Deities enshrined: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto]], Kukuno-no-Mikoto, [[Kunisatsuchi|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto]], [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto]], [[Mizuhanome|Mizuhanome-no-Mikoto]] * Shrine Office * Miyashige [[Inari Shrine]] - Deities enshrined: [[Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ebisu|Hiruko-no-Ōkami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Ōkami]] File:Main shrine of Okazaki-jinja.jpg|Many visitors come during the Year of the Rabbit (2023) File:岡崎神社 - 入口石鳥居.jpg|Stone torii at the entrance File:岡崎神社 - 手水舎.JPG|Chozuya (water purification basin) File:岡崎神社 - 宮繁稲荷神社.JPG|Miyashige Inari Shrine File:岡崎神社 - 狛うさぎ1.JPG|Koma-usagi (guardian rabbits) File:Rabbit statue of Okazaki-jinja 001.jpg|Child-granting rabbit statue == Access== === Bus Routes=== * [[Kyoto City Bus]] ''' "Okazaki Shrine-mae" Bus Stop (Routes 32, 93, 203, 204) {{Cite web|url=https://www2.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kotsu/busdia/hyperdia/menu463.htm |title=Okazaki Shrine-mae |publisher=Kyoto City Office |accessdate=2023-03-22}} === Nearest Train Stations=== * [[Kyoto Municipal Subway Tōzai Line]] - [[Keage Station]] * [[Keihan Electric Railway]] - [[Jingū-Marutamachi Station]] {{Shinto navigation}} [[ja:%E5%B2%A1%E5%B4%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]][[Category:U]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category:ted completion categories]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okazaki Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okazaki Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okazaki Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okazaki Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Okazaki Shrine]][[Category:Okazaki Shrine]][[Category:Okazaki Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Okazaki Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Okazaki Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Okazaki Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Okazaki Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Okazaki Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Okazaki Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Okazaki Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Okazaki Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Okazaki Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Okazaki Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:hashtag replacing]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okazaki Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okazaki Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Okazaki Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okazaki Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[snOkazaki Shrine]] [[Category:New]]

Historically, there were more than ten large decorative floats (hoko), but they no longer participate in the festival.{{Cite book|author=Takashi Sawa, et al. |title=Kyoto Encyclopedia |publisher=Tankosha |year=1984 |page=135 |isbn=4-473-00885-1}}{{Cite book|author=Itsuo Munemasa, Katsuhisa Moriya |title=Kyoto Saijiki |publisher=Tankosha |year=1986 |page=262 |isbn=4-473-00940-8}} * [[November 16]] - Fire Ritual Festival (Hitaki-sai) * [[November 23]] - Niiname-sai (Harvest Festival) * [[December 2]] - Ame-no-Yashiro Festival * [[December 31]] - Toshikoshi-no-Harae (Year-End Purification) == Enshrined Deities== * Main Deities - {{otheruses|Shinto shrine in Kyoto, Japan|Shinto shrine in Hiroshima, Japan|Okazaki Shrine (Hiroshima)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |Name = Okazaki Shrine |image = [[File:Okazaki-jinja haiden.jpg|260px]]
Haiden |Location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Sakyo Ward]], [[Okazaki (Kyoto)|Okazaki]] Higashitenno-cho 51
{{Cite web|url=http://www.okazakijinja.jp/access/|title=Access|publisher=Okazaki Shrine|accessdate=2017-01-25|ref=}} |Coordinates={{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point|frame-width=200}} |Deity = [[Susanoo|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]]
[[Gonansanjoshin|Three Female and Five Male Deities]] |Shintai = |Shrine rank = Former [[Gōsha|District Shrine]] |Established = [[Enryaku]] 13 (794 AD) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Other names = Higashitenno-sha, Higashitenno |Pilgrimage = [[Kyoto Jurokushajin]]
[[Zenkoku Seiseikai]] (59 Shrines Enshrining Susanoo-no-Mikoto) |Annual festival = [[July 16]] (Summer Purification Festival) |Rituals = |Map = {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point|frame-width=200}} }} {{osm box|w|1155362848}} '''Okazaki Shrine''' (岡崎神社, Okazaki Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Okazaki (Kyoto)|Okazaki Higashitenno-cho]], [[Sakyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]]. It is also known as "Higashitenno." Since the surrounding area was once inhabited by [[wild rabbits]],{{Cite news|title=Full of rabbits – overcoming hardships with leaps and bounds |newspaper=Asahi Shimbun |page=31 |date=2023-01-16 |author=}} rabbits are considered the shrine’s [[Ujigami|guardian spirits]] and [[shinshi|divine messengers]]. == Overview== Besides being known as a deity of safe childbirth, the shrine is also believed to grant fertility, as rabbits are known for their prolific reproduction. Additionally, its enshrined deities, [[Susanoo|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Kushinadahime]], are associated with having many children. The shrine is also regarded as a place of [[katatagae|directional protection]] due to its historical origins. Several rabbit stone statues can be found throughout the precincts. == History== In [[Enryaku]] 13 (794 AD), during Emperor [[Kanmu]]’s relocation of the capital to [[Heian-kyō]], the shrine was established as one of the [[Taishogun-sha]] (Great General Shrines) built at the four corners of the city to protect the capital. Since it was located to the east, it became known as "Higashitenno."{{Cite book|author= |title=Kyoto Encyclopedia |publisher=Naoya Kaji |date=1984-11-12 |page=135 |isbn=4-473-00885-1}} In [[Jogan]] 11 (869 AD), Emperor [[Seiwa]] ordered the construction of the shrine, enshrining [[Gozu Tenno]] (identified with [[Susanoo]]) from [[Hiromine Shrine]] in [[Harima Province]]. At the time, it served as the guardian shrine of [[Tokoji Temple]], which was located in the same area. In [[Jisho]] 2 (1178 AD), Emperor [[Takakura]] dedicated offerings for the safe childbirth of his co]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category:t, [[Taira no Tokuko]]. In [[Gen'ō]] 1 (1319 AD), Emperor [[Go-Daigo]] rebuilt the shrine and granted it the rank of Shoichii (Senior First Rank) along with sacred treasures. During the [[Ōnin War]], the temple was destroyed by fire, leaving only the shrine intact.{{Cite book|title=Showa Kyoto Guidebook |publisher=Obuchi Kaoru |date=1981-10-30 |page=179}} During the [[Keichō era]] (1596–1615), the shrine was renamed to its current name. In the [[Meiji era]], it was designated as a [[Gōsha|District Shrine]]. On [[September 21, 1934]], the [[Muroto Typhoon]] caused strong winds that collapsed the Haiden (worship hall).Many were trapped under collapsed school buildings in Kyoto. ''Osaka Mainichi Shimbun'' (September 21, 1934). ''Showa News Encyclopedia, Vol. 4 (Showa 8–9)''. == Annual Festivals== A signboard at the entrance lists the annual festivals as follows: * [[January 1]] - New Year's Festival (Saitan-sai) * [[January 16]] - Miyashige Inari Festival / Senbo Festival * [[February 3]] - Setsubun Festival * [[March 16]] - Kinensai (Prayer Festival) * [[March]] (Spring Equinox) - Spring Ancestral Festival * [[May 16]] - Senbo Festival * [[June 30]] - Nagoshi-no-Harae (Summer Purification) * [[July 16]] - Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * [[September 16]] - Senbo Festival * [[September]] (Autumn Equinox) - Autumn Ancestral Festival * [[October 16]] - Parishioners' Festival ** Includes a children's procession, children's portable shrine (mikoshi), and hot-water purification ritual (Yugama). Also known as the Okazaki Tenno Festival, Okazaki Festival, or Grand Festival. Historically, there were more than ten large decorative floats (hoko), but they no longer participate in the festival.{{Cite book|author=Takashi Sawa, et al. |title=Kyoto Encyclopedia |publisher=Tankosha |year=1984 |page=135 |isbn=4-473-00885-1}}{{Cite book|author=Itsuo Munemasa, Katsuhisa Moriya |title=Kyoto Saijiki |publisher=Tankosha |year=1986 |page=262 |isbn=4-473-00940-8}} * [[November 16]] - Fire Ritual Festival (Hitaki-sai) * [[November 23]] - Niiname-sai (Harvest Festival) * [[December 2]] - Ame-no-Yashiro Festival * [[December 31]] - Toshikoshi-no-Harae (Year-End Purification) == Enshrined Deities== * Main Deities - [[Susanoo|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Kushinadahime]], [[Gonansanjoshin|Three Female and Five Male Deities]] == Shrine Grounds== * Main Hall * Worship Hall * Ame-no-Yashiro - Deities enshrined: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto]], Kukuno-no-Mikoto, [[Kunisatsuchi|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto]], [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto]], [[Mizuhanome|Mizuhanome-no-Mikoto]] * Shrine Office * Miyashige [[Inari Shrine]] - Deities enshrined: [[Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ebisu|Hiruko-no-Ōkami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Ōkami]] File:Main shrine of Okazaki-jinja.jpg|Many visitors come during the Year of the Rabbit (2023) File:岡崎神社 - Also known as the Okazaki Tenno Festival, Okazaki Festival, or Grand Festival. {{otheruses|Shinto shrine in Kyoto, Japan|Shinto shrine in Hiroshima, Japan|Okazaki Shrine (Hiroshima)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |Name = Okazaki Shrine |image = [[File:Okazaki-jinja haiden.jpg|260px]]
Haiden |Location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Sakyo Ward]], [[Okazaki (Kyoto)|Okazaki]] Higashitenno-cho 51
{{Cite web|url=http://www.okazakijinja.jp/access/|title=Access|publisher=Okazaki Shrine|accessdate=2017-01-25|ref=}} |Coordinates={{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point|frame-width=200}} |Deity = [[Susanoo|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]]
[[Gonansanjoshin|Three Female and Five Male Deities]] |Shintai = |Shrine rank = Former [[Gōsha|District Shrine]] |Established = [[Enryaku]] 13 (794 AD) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Other names = Higashitenno-sha, Higashitenno |Pilgrimage = [[Kyoto Jurokushajin]]
[[Zenkoku Seiseikai]] (59 Shrines Enshrining Susanoo-no-Mikoto) |Annual festival = [[July 16]] (Summer Purification Festival) |Rituals = |Map = {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point|frame-width=200}} }} {{osm box|w|1155362848}} '''Okazaki Shrine''' (岡崎神社, Okazaki Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Okazaki (Kyoto)|Okazaki Higashitenno-cho]], [[Sakyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]]. It is also known as "Higashitenno." Since the surrounding area was once inhabited by [[wild rabbits]],{{Cite news|title=Full of rabbits – overcoming hardships with leaps and bounds |newspaper=Asahi Shimbun |page=31 |date=2023-01-16 |author=}} rabbits are considered the shrine’s [[Ujigami|guardian spirits]] and [[shinshi|divine messengers]]. == Overview== Besides being known as a deity of safe childbirth, the shrine is also believed to grant fertility, as rabbits are known for their prolific reproduction. Additionally, its enshrined deities, [[Susanoo|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Kushinadahime]], are associated with having many children. The shrine is also regarded as a place of [[katatagae|directional protection]] due to its historical origins. Several rabbit stone statues can be found throughout the precincts. == History== In [[Enryaku]] 13 (794 AD), during Emperor [[Kanmu]]’s relocation of the capital to [[Heian-kyō]], the shrine was established as one of the [[Taishogun-sha]] (Great General Shrines) built at the four corners of the city to protect the capital. Since it was located to the east, it became known as "Higashitenno."{{Cite book|author= |title=Kyoto Encyclopedia |publisher=Naoya Kaji |date=1984-11-12 |page=135 |isbn=4-473-00885-1}} In [[Jogan]] 11 (869 AD), Emperor [[Seiwa]] ordered the construction of the shrine, enshrining [[Gozu Tenno]] (identified with [[Susanoo]]) from [[Hiromine Shrine]] in [[Harima Province]]. At the time, it served as the guardian shrine of [[Tokoji Temple]], which was located in the same area. In [[Jisho]] 2 (1178 AD), Emperor [[Takakura]] dedicated offerings for the safe childbirth of his co]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category:t, [[Taira no Tokuko]]. In [[Gen'ō]] 1 (1319 AD), Emperor [[Go-Daigo]] rebuilt the shrine and granted it the rank of Shoichii (Senior First Rank) along with sacred treasures. During the [[Ōnin War]], the temple was destroyed by fire, leaving only the shrine intact.{{Cite book|title=Showa Kyoto Guidebook |publisher=Obuchi Kaoru |date=1981-10-30 |page=179}} During the [[Keichō era]] (1596–1615), the shrine was renamed to its current name. In the [[Meiji era]], it was designated as a [[Gōsha|District Shrine]]. On [[September 21, 1934]], the [[Muroto Typhoon]] caused strong winds that collapsed the Haiden (worship hall).Many were trapped under collapsed school buildings in Kyoto. ''Osaka Mainichi Shimbun'' (September 21, 1934). ''Showa News Encyclopedia, Vol. 4 (Showa 8–9)''. == Annual Festivals== A signboard at the entrance lists the annual festivals as follows: * [[January 1]] - New Year's Festival (Saitan-sai) * [[January 16]] - Miyashige Inari Festival / Senbo Festival * [[February 3]] - Setsubun Festival * [[March 16]] - Kinensai (Prayer Festival) * [[March]] (Spring Equinox) - Spring Ancestral Festival * [[May 16]] - Senbo Festival * [[June 30]] - Nagoshi-no-Harae (Summer Purification) * [[July 16]] - Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * [[September 16]] - Senbo Festival * [[September]] (Autumn Equinox) - Autumn Ancestral Festival * [[October 16]] - Parishioners' Festival ** Includes a children's procession, children's portable shrine (mikoshi), and hot-water purification ritual (Yugama). Also known as the Okazaki Tenno Festival, Okazaki Festival, or Grand Festival. Historically, there were more than ten large decorative floats (hoko), but they no longer participate in the festival.{{Cite book|author=Takashi Sawa, et al. |title=Kyoto Encyclopedia |publisher=Tankosha |year=1984 |page=135 |isbn=4-473-00885-1}}{{Cite book|author=Itsuo Munemasa, Katsuhisa Moriya |title=Kyoto Saijiki |publisher=Tankosha |year=1986 |page=262 |isbn=4-473-00940-8}} * [[November 16]] - Fire Ritual Festival (Hitaki-sai) * [[November 23]] - Niiname-sai (Harvest Festival) * [[December 2]] - Ame-no-Yashiro Festival * [[December 31]] - Toshikoshi-no-Harae (Year-End Purification) == Enshrined Deities== * Main Deities - [[Susanoo|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Kushinadahime]], [[Gonansanjoshin|Three Female and Five Male Deities]] == Shrine Grounds== * Main Hall * Worship Hall * Ame-no-Yashiro - Deities enshrined: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto]], Kukuno-no-Mikoto, [[Kunisatsuchi|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto]], [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto]], [[Mizuhanome|Mizuhanome-no-Mikoto]] * Shrine Office * Miyashige [[Inari Shrine]] - Deities enshrined: [[Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ebisu|Hiruko-no-Ōkami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Ōkami]] File:Main shrine of Okazaki-jinja.jpg|Many visitors come during the Year of the Rabbit (2023) File:岡崎神社 - ''Showa News Encyclopedia, Vol. 4 (Showa 8–9)''. == Annual Festivals== A signboard at the entrance lists the annual festivals as follows: * [[January 1]] - New Year's Festival (Saitan-sai) * [[January 16]] - Miyashige Inari Festival / Senbo Festival * [[February 3]] - Setsubun Festival * [[March 16]] - Kinensai (Prayer Festival) * [[March]] (Spring Equinox) - Spring Ancestral Festival * [[May 16]] - Senbo Festival * [[June 30]] - Nagoshi-no-Harae (Summer Purification) * [[July 16]] - Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * [[September 16]] - Senbo Festival * [[September]] (Autumn Equinox) - Autumn Ancestral Festival * [[October 16]] - Parishioners' Festival ** Includes a children's procession, children's portable shrine (mikoshi), and hot-water purification ritual (Yugama). Also known as the Okazaki Tenno Festival, Okazaki Festival, or Grand Festival. Historically, there were more than ten large decorative floats (hoko), but they no longer participate in the festival.{{Cite book|author=Takashi Sawa, et al. |title=Kyoto Encyclopedia |publisher=Tankosha |year=1984 |page=135 |isbn=4-473-00885-1}}{{Cite book|author=Itsuo Munemasa, Katsuhisa Moriya |title=Kyoto Saijiki |publisher=Tankosha |year=1986 |page=262 |isbn=4-473-00940-8}} * [[November 16]] - Fire Ritual Festival (Hitaki-sai) * [[November 23]] - Niiname-sai (Harvest Festival) * [[December 2]] - Ame-no-Yashiro Festival * [[December 31]] - Toshikoshi-no-Harae (Year-End Purification) == Enshrined Deities== * Main Deities - [[Susanoo|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Kushinadahime]], [[Gonansanjoshin|Three Female and Five Male Deities]] == Shrine Grounds== * Main Hall * Worship Hall * Ame-no-Yashiro - Deities enshrined: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto]], Kukuno-no-Mikoto, [[Kunisatsuchi|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto]], [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto]], [[Mizuhanome|Mizuhanome-no-Mikoto]] * Shrine Office * Miyashige [[Inari Shrine]] - Deities enshrined: [[Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ebisu|Hiruko-no-Ōkami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Ōkami]] File:Main shrine of Okazaki-jinja.jpg|Many visitors come during the Year of the Rabbit (2023) File:岡崎神社 - {{otheruses|Shinto shrine in Kyoto, Japan|Shinto shrine in Hiroshima, Japan|Okazaki Shrine (Hiroshima)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |Name = Okazaki Shrine |image = [[File:Okazaki-jinja haiden.jpg|260px]]
Haiden |Location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Sakyo Ward]], [[Okazaki (Kyoto)|Okazaki]] Higashitenno-cho 51
{{Cite web|url=http://www.okazakijinja.jp/access/|title=Access|publisher=Okazaki Shrine|accessdate=2017-01-25|ref=}} |Coordinates={{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point|frame-width=200}} |Deity = [[Susanoo|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]]
[[Gonansanjoshin|Three Female and Five Male Deities]] |Shintai = |Shrine rank = Former [[Gōsha|District Shrine]] |Established = [[Enryaku]] 13 (794 AD) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Other names = Higashitenno-sha, Higashitenno |Pilgrimage = [[Kyoto Jurokushajin]]
[[Zenkoku Seiseikai]] (59 Shrines Enshrining Susanoo-no-Mikoto) |Annual festival = [[July 16]] (Summer Purification Festival) |Rituals = |Map = {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point|frame-width=200}} }} {{osm box|w|1155362848}} '''Okazaki Shrine''' (岡崎神社, Okazaki Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Okazaki (Kyoto)|Okazaki Higashitenno-cho]], [[Sakyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]]. It is also known as "Higashitenno." Since the surrounding area was once inhabited by [[wild rabbits]],{{Cite news|title=Full of rabbits – overcoming hardships with leaps and bounds |newspaper=Asahi Shimbun |page=31 |date=2023-01-16 |author=}} rabbits are considered the shrine’s [[Ujigami|guardian spirits]] and [[shinshi|divine messengers]]. == Overview== Besides being known as a deity of safe childbirth, the shrine is also believed to grant fertility, as rabbits are known for their prolific reproduction. Additionally, its enshrined deities, [[Susanoo|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Kushinadahime]], are associated with having many children. The shrine is also regarded as a place of [[katatagae|directional protection]] due to its historical origins. Several rabbit stone statues can be found throughout the precincts. == History== In [[Enryaku]] 13 (794 AD), during Emperor [[Kanmu]]’s relocation of the capital to [[Heian-kyō]], the shrine was established as one of the [[Taishogun-sha]] (Great General Shrines) built at the four corners of the city to protect the capital. Since it was located to the east, it became known as "Higashitenno."{{Cite book|author= |title=Kyoto Encyclopedia |publisher=Naoya Kaji |date=1984-11-12 |page=135 |isbn=4-473-00885-1}} In [[Jogan]] 11 (869 AD), Emperor [[Seiwa]] ordered the construction of the shrine, enshrining [[Gozu Tenno]] (identified with [[Susanoo]]) from [[Hiromine Shrine]] in [[Harima Province]]. At the time, it served as the guardian shrine of [[Tokoji Temple]], which was located in the same area. In [[Jisho]] 2 (1178 AD), Emperor [[Takakura]] dedicated offerings for the safe childbirth of his co]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:t, [[Taira no Tokuko]]. In [[Gen'ō]] 1 (1319 AD), Emperor [[Go-Daigo]] rebuilt the shrine and granted it the rank of Shoichii (Senior First Rank) along with sacred treasures. During the [[Ōnin War]], the temple was destroyed by fire, leaving only the shrine intact.{{Cite book|title=Showa Kyoto Guidebook |publisher=Obuchi Kaoru |date=1981-10-30 |page=179}} During the [[Keichō era]] (1596–1615), the shrine was renamed to its current name. In the [[Meiji era]], it was designated as a [[Gōsha|District Shrine]]. On [[September 21, 1934]], the [[Muroto Typhoon]] caused strong winds that collapsed the Haiden (worship hall).Many were trapped under collapsed school buildings in Kyoto. ''Osaka Mainichi Shimbun'' (September 21, 1934). ''Showa News Encyclopedia, Vol. 4 (Showa 8–9)''. == Annual Festivals== A signboard at the entrance lists the annual festivals as follows: * [[January 1]] - New Year's Festival (Saitan-sai) * [[January 16]] - Miyashige Inari Festival / Senbo Festival * [[February 3]] - Setsubun Festival * [[March 16]] - Kinensai (Prayer Festival) * [[March]] (Spring Equinox) - Spring Ancestral Festival * [[May 16]] - Senbo Festival * [[June 30]] - Nagoshi-no-Harae (Summer Purification) * [[July 16]] - Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * [[September 16]] - Senbo Festival * [[September]] (Autumn Equinox) - Autumn Ancestral Festival * [[October 16]] - Parishioners' Festival ** Includes a children's procession, children's portable shrine (mikoshi), and hot-water purification ritual (Yugama). Also known as the Okazaki Tenno Festival, Okazaki Festival, or Grand Festival. Historically, there were more than ten large decorative floats (hoko), but they no longer participate in the festival.{{Cite book|author=Takashi Sawa, et al. |title=Kyoto Encyclopedia |publisher=Tankosha |year=1984 |page=135 |isbn=4-473-00885-1}}{{Cite book|author=Itsuo Munemasa, Katsuhisa Moriya |title=Kyoto Saijiki |publisher=Tankosha |year=1986 |page=262 |isbn=4-473-00940-8}} * [[November 16]] - Fire Ritual Festival (Hitaki-sai) * [[November 23]] - Niiname-sai (Harvest Festival) * [[December 2]] - Ame-no-Yashiro Festival * [[December 31]] - Toshikoshi-no-Harae (Year-End Purification) == Enshrined Deities== * Main Deities - [[Susanoo|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Kushinadahime]], [[Gonansanjoshin|Three Female and Five Male Deities]] == Shrine Grounds== * Main Hall * Worship Hall * Ame-no-Yashiro - Deities enshrined: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto]], Kukuno-no-Mikoto, [[Kunisatsuchi|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto]], [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto]], [[Mizuhanome|Mizuhanome-no-Mikoto]] * Shrine Office * Miyashige [[Inari Shrine]] - Deities enshrined: [[Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ebisu|Hiruko-no-Ōkami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Ōkami]] File:Main shrine of Okazaki-jinja.jpg|Many visitors come during the Year of the Rabbit (2023) File:岡崎神社 - 入口石鳥居.jpg|Stone torii at the entrance File:岡崎神社 - 手水舎.JPG|Chozuya (water purification basin) File:岡崎神社 - 宮繁稲荷神社.JPG|Miyashige Inari Shrine File:岡崎神社 - 狛うさぎ1.JPG|Koma-usagi (guardian rabbits) File:Rabbit statue of Okazaki-jinja 001.jpg|Child-granting rabbit statue == Access== === Bus Routes=== * [[Kyoto City Bus]] ''' "Okazaki Shrine-mae" Bus Stop (Routes 32, 93, 203, 204) {{Cite web|url=https://www2.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kotsu/busdia/hyperdia/menu463.htm |title=Okazaki Shrine-mae |publisher=Kyoto City Office |accessdate=2023-03-22}} === Nearest Train Stations=== * [[Kyoto Municipal Subway Tōzai Line]] - [[Keage Station]] * [[Keihan Electric Railway]] - [[Jingū-Marutamachi Station]] {{Shinto navigation}} [[ja:%E5%B2%A1%E5%B4%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]][[Category:completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:ted completion tier 5]][[Category: tier 5]][[Category:11]][[sn:Okazaki Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okazaki Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okazaki Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okazaki Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Okazaki Shrine]][[Category:Okazaki Shrine]][[Category:Okazaki Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Okazaki Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Okazaki Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Okazaki Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Okazaki Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Okazaki Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Okazaki Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Okazaki Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Okazaki Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Okazaki Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Okazaki Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okazaki Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okazaki Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Okazaki Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Okazaki Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[snOkazaki Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

{{otheruses|Shinto shrine in Kyoto, Japan|Shinto shrine in Hiroshima, Japan|Okazaki Shrine (Hiroshima)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |Name = Okazaki Shrine |image = [[File:Okazaki-jinja haiden.jpg|260px]]
Haiden |Location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Sakyo Ward]], [[Okazaki (Kyoto)|Okazaki]] Higashitenno-cho 51
{{Cite web|url=http://www.okazakijinja.jp/access/|title=Access|publisher=Okazaki Shrine|accessdate=2017-01-25|ref=}} |Coordinates={{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point|frame-width=200}} |Deity = [[Susanoo|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]]
[[Gonansanjoshin|Three Female and Five Male Deities]] |Shintai = |Shrine rank = Former [[Gōsha|District Shrine]] |Established = [[Enryaku]] 13 (794 AD) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Other names = Higashitenno-sha, Higashitenno |Pilgrimage = [[Kyoto Jurokushajin]]
[[Zenkoku Seiseikai]] (59 Shrines Enshrining Susanoo-no-Mikoto) |Annual festival = [[July 16]] (Summer Purification Festival) |Rituals = |Map = {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point|frame-width=200}} }} {{osm box|w|1155362848}} '''Okazaki Shrine''' (岡崎神社, Okazaki Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Okazaki (Kyoto)|Okazaki Higashitenno-cho]], [[Sakyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]]. It is also known as "Higashitenno." Since the surrounding area was once inhabited by [[wild rabbits]],{{Cite news|title=Full of rabbits – overcoming hardships with leaps and bounds |newspaper=Asahi Shimbun |page=31 |date=2023-01-16 |author=}} rabbits are considered the shrine’s [[Ujigami|guardian spirits]] and [[shinshi|divine messengers]]. == Overview== Besides being known as a deity of safe childbirth, the shrine is also believed to grant fertility, as rabbits are known for their prolific reproduction. Additionally, its enshrined deities, [[Susanoo|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Kushinadahime]], are associated with having many children. The shrine is also regarded as a place of [[katatagae|directional protection]] due to its historical origins. Several rabbit stone statues can be found throughout the precincts. == History== In [[Enryaku]] 13 (794 AD), during Emperor [[Kanmu]]’s relocation of the capital to [[Heian-kyō]], the shrine was established as one of the [[Taishogun-sha]] (Great General Shrines) built at the four corners of the city to protect the capital. Since it was located to the east, it became known as "Higashitenno."{{Cite book|author= |title=Kyoto Encyclopedia |publisher=Naoya Kaji |date=1984-11-12 |page=135 |isbn=4-473-00885-1}} In [[Jogan]] 11 (869 AD), Emperor [[Seiwa]] ordered the construction of the shrine, enshrining [[Gozu Tenno]] (identified with [[Susanoo]]) from [[Hiromine Shrine]] in [[Harima Province]]. At the time, it served as the guardian shrine of [[Tokoji Temple]], which was located in the same area. In [[Jisho]] 2 (1178 AD), Emperor [[Takakura]] dedicated offerings for the safe childbirth of his co]]. In [[Gen'ō]] 1 (1319 AD), Emperor [[Go-Daigo]] rebuilt the shrine and granted it the rank of Shoichii (Senior First Rank) along with sacred treasures. During the [[Ōnin War]], the temple was destroyed by fire, leaving only the shrine intact.{{Cite book|title=Showa Kyoto Guidebook |publisher=Obuchi Kaoru |date=1981-10-30 |page=179}} During the [[Keichō era]] (1596–1615), the shrine was renamed to its current name. In the [[Meiji era]], it was designated as a [[Gōsha|District Shrine]]. On [[September 21, 1934]], the [[Muroto Typhoon]] caused strong winds that collapsed the Haiden (worship hall).Many were trapped under collapsed school buildings in Kyoto. ''Osaka Mainichi Shimbun'' (September 21, 1934). ''Showa News Encyclopedia, Vol. 4 (Showa 8–9)''. == Annual Festivals== A signboard at the entrance lists the annual festivals as follows: * [[January 1]] - New Year's Festival (Saitan-sai) * [[January 16]] - Miyashige Inari Festival / Senbo Festival * [[February 3]] - Setsubun Festival * [[March 16]] - Kinensai (Prayer Festival) * [[March]] (Spring Equinox) - Spring Ancestral Festival * [[May 16]] - Senbo Festival * [[June 30]] - Nagoshi-no-Harae (Summer Purification) * [[July 16]] - Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * [[September 16]] - Senbo Festival * [[September]] (Autumn Equinox) - Autumn Ancestral Festival * [[October 16]] - Parishioners' Festival ** Includes a children's procession, children's portable shrine (mikoshi), and hot-water purification ritual (Yugama). Also known as the Okazaki Tenno Festival, Okazaki Festival, or Grand Festival. Historically, there were more than ten large decorative floats (hoko), but they no longer participate in the festival.{{Cite book|author=Takashi Sawa, et al. |title=Kyoto Encyclopedia |publisher=Tankosha |year=1984 |page=135 |isbn=4-473-00885-1}}{{Cite book|author=Itsuo Munemasa, Katsuhisa Moriya |title=Kyoto Saijiki |publisher=Tankosha |year=1986 |page=262 |isbn=4-473-00940-8}} * [[November 16]] - Fire Ritual Festival (Hitaki-sai) * [[November 23]] - Niiname-sai (Harvest Festival) * [[December 2]] - Ame-no-Yashiro Festival * [[December 31]] - Toshikoshi-no-Harae (Year-End Purification) == Enshrined Deities== * Main Deities - [[Susanoo|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Kushinadahime]], [[Gonansanjoshin|Three Female and Five Male Deities]] == Shrine Grounds== * Main Hall * Worship Hall * Ame-no-Yashiro - Deities enshrined: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto]], Kukuno-no-Mikoto, [[Kunisatsuchi|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto]], [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto]], [[Mizuhanome|Mizuhanome-no-Mikoto]] * Shrine Office * Miyashige [[Inari Shrine]] - Deities enshrined: [[Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ebisu|Hiruko-no-Ōkami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Ōkami]] File:Main shrine of Okazaki-jinja.jpg|Many visitors come during the Year of the Rabbit (2023) File:岡崎神社 - 入口石鳥居.jpg|Stone torii at the entrance File:岡崎神社 - 手水舎.JPG|Chozuya (water purification basin) File:岡崎神社 - 宮繁稲荷神社.JPG|Miyashige Inari Shrine File:岡崎神社 - 狛うさぎ1.JPG|Koma-usagi (guardian rabbits) File:Rabbit statue of Okazaki-jinja 001.jpg|Child-granting rabbit statue == Access== === Bus Routes=== * [[Kyoto City Bus]] ''' "Okazaki Shrine-mae" Bus Stop (Routes 32, 93, 203, 204) {{Cite web|url=https://www2.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kotsu/busdia/hyperdia/menu463.htm |title=Okazaki Shrine-mae |publisher=Kyoto City Office |accessdate=2023-03-22}} === Nearest Train Stations=== * [[Kyoto Municipal Subway Tōzai Line]] - revision.text:
{{otheruses|Shinto shrine in Kyoto, Japan|Shinto shrine in Hiroshima, Japan|Okazaki Shrine (Hiroshima)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |Name = Okazaki Shrine |image = [[File:Okazaki-jinja haiden.jpg|260px]]
Haiden |Location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Sakyo Ward]], [[Okazaki (Kyoto)|Okazaki]] Higashitenno-cho 51
{{Cite web|url=http://www.okazakijinja.jp/access/|title=Access|publisher=Okazaki Shrine|accessdate=2017-01-25|ref=}} |Coordinates={{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point|frame-width=200}} |Deity = [[Susanoo|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]]
[[Gonansanjoshin|Three Female and Five Male Deities]] |Shintai = |Shrine rank = Former [[Gōsha|District Shrine]] |Established = [[Enryaku]] 13 (794 AD) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Other names = Higashitenno-sha, Higashitenno |Pilgrimage = [[Kyoto Jurokushajin]]
[[Zenkoku Seiseikai]] (59 Shrines Enshrining Susanoo-no-Mikoto) |Annual festival = [[July 16]] (Summer Purification Festival) |Rituals = |Map = {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point|frame-width=200}} }} {{osm box|w|1155362848}} '''Okazaki Shrine''' (岡崎神社, Okazaki Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Okazaki (Kyoto)|Okazaki Higashitenno-cho]], [[Sakyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]]. It is also known as "Higashitenno." Since the surrounding area was once inhabited by [[wild rabbits]],{{Cite news|title=Full of rabbits – overcoming hardships with leaps and bounds |newspaper=Asahi Shimbun |page=31 |date=2023-01-16 |author=}} rabbits are considered the shrine’s [[Ujigami|guardian spirits]] and [[shinshi|divine messengers]]. == Overview== Besides being known as a deity of safe childbirth, the shrine is also believed to grant fertility, as rabbits are known for their prolific reproduction. Additionally, its enshrined deities, [[Susanoo|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Kushinadahime]], are associated with having many children. The shrine is also regarded as a place of [[katatagae|directional protection]] due to its historical origins. Several rabbit stone statues can be found throughout the precincts. == History== In [[Enryaku]] 13 (794 AD), during Emperor [[Kanmu]]’s relocation of the capital to [[Heian-kyō]], the shrine was established as one of the [[Taishogun-sha]] (Great General Shrines) built at the four corners of the city to protect the capital. Since it was located to the east, it became known as "Higashitenno."{{Cite book|author= |title=Kyoto Encyclopedia |publisher=Naoya Kaji |date=1984-11-12 |page=135 |isbn=4-473-00885-1}} In [[Jogan]] 11 (869 AD), Emperor [[Seiwa]] ordered the construction of the shrine, enshrining [[Gozu Tenno]] (identified with [[Susanoo]]) from [[Hiromine Shrine]] in [[Harima Province]]. At the time, it served as the guardian shrine of [[Tokoji Temple]], which was located in the same area. In [[Jisho]] 2 (1178 AD), Emperor [[Takakura]] dedicated offerings for the safe childbirth of his co]]. In [[Gen'ō]] 1 (1319 AD), Emperor [[Go-Daigo]] rebuilt the shrine and granted it the rank of Shoichii (Senior First Rank) along with sacred treasures. During the [[Ōnin War]], the temple was destroyed by fire, leaving only the shrine intact.{{Cite book|title=Showa Kyoto Guidebook |publisher=Obuchi Kaoru |date=1981-10-30 |page=179}} During the [[Keichō era]] (1596–1615), the shrine was renamed to its current name. In the [[Meiji era]], it was designated as a [[Gōsha|District Shrine]]. On [[September 21, 1934]], the [[Muroto Typhoon]] caused strong winds that collapsed the Haiden (worship hall).Many were trapped under collapsed school buildings in Kyoto. ''Osaka Mainichi Shimbun'' (September 21, 1934). ''Showa News Encyclopedia, Vol. 4 (Showa 8–9)''. == Annual Festivals== A signboard at the entrance lists the annual festivals as follows: * [[January 1]] - New Year's Festival (Saitan-sai) * [[January 16]] - Miyashige Inari Festival / Senbo Festival * [[February 3]] - Setsubun Festival * [[March 16]] - Kinensai (Prayer Festival) * [[March]] (Spring Equinox) - Spring Ancestral Festival * [[May 16]] - Senbo Festival * [[June 30]] - Nagoshi-no-Harae (Summer Purification) * [[July 16]] - Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * [[September 16]] - Senbo Festival * [[September]] (Autumn Equinox) - Autumn Ancestral Festival * [[October 16]] - Parishioners' Festival ** Includes a children's procession, children's portable shrine (mikoshi), and hot-water purification ritual (Yugama). Also known as the Okazaki Tenno Festival, Okazaki Festival, or Grand Festival. Historically, there were more than ten large decorative floats (hoko), but they no longer participate in the festival.{{Cite book|author=Takashi Sawa, et al. |title=Kyoto Encyclopedia |publisher=Tankosha |year=1984 |page=135 |isbn=4-473-00885-1}}{{Cite book|author=Itsuo Munemasa, Katsuhisa Moriya |title=Kyoto Saijiki |publisher=Tankosha |year=1986 |page=262 |isbn=4-473-00940-8}} * [[November 16]] - Fire Ritual Festival (Hitaki-sai) * [[November 23]] - Niiname-sai (Harvest Festival) * [[December 2]] - Ame-no-Yashiro Festival * [[December 31]] - Toshikoshi-no-Harae (Year-End Purification) == Enshrined Deities== * Main Deities - [[Susanoo|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Kushinadahime]], [[Gonansanjoshin|Three Female and Five Male Deities]] == Shrine Grounds== * Main Hall * Worship Hall * Ame-no-Yashiro - Deities enshrined: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto]], Kukuno-no-Mikoto, [[Kunisatsuchi|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto]], [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto]], [[Mizuhanome|Mizuhanome-no-Mikoto]] * Shrine Office * Miyashige [[Inari Shrine]] - Deities enshrined: [[Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ebisu|Hiruko-no-Ōkami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Ōkami]] File:Main shrine of Okazaki-jinja.jpg|Many visitors come during the Year of the Rabbit (2023) File:岡崎神社 - 入口石鳥居.jpg|Stone torii at the entrance File:岡崎神社 - 手水舎.JPG|Chozuya (water purification basin) File:岡崎神社 - 宮繁稲荷神社.JPG|Miyashige Inari Shrine File:岡崎神社 - 狛うさぎ1.JPG|Koma-usagi (guardian rabbits) File:Rabbit statue of Okazaki-jinja 001.jpg|Child-granting rabbit statue == Access== === Bus Routes=== * [[Kyoto City Bus]] ''' "Okazaki Shrine-mae" Bus Stop (Routes 32, 93, 203, 204) {{Cite web|url=https://www2.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kotsu/busdia/hyperdia/menu463.htm |title=Okazaki Shrine-mae |publisher=Kyoto City Office |accessdate=2023-03-22}} === Nearest Train Stations=== * [[Kyoto Municipal Subway Tōzai Line]] - [[Keage Station]] * [[Keihan Electric Railway]] - [[Jingū-Marutamachi Station]] {{Shinto navigation}} [[snOkazaki Shrine]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:t, [[Taira no Tokuko]] [[Category:ted completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Okazaki Shrine]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:%E5%B2%A1%E5%B4%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Okazaki Shrine]] [[en:Okazaki Shrine]]

Additionally, its enshrined deities, [[Susanoo|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Kushinadahime]], are associated with having many children. The shrine is also regarded as a place of [[katatagae|directional protection]] due to its historical origins. Several rabbit stone statues can be found throughout the precincts. == History== In [[Enryaku]] 13 (794 AD), during Emperor [[Kanmu]]’s relocation of the capital to [[Heian-kyō]], the shrine was established as one of the [[Taishogun-sha]] (Great General Shrines) built at the four corners of the city to protect the capital. Since it was located to the east, it became known as "Higashitenno."{{Cite book|author= |title=Kyoto Encyclopedia |publisher=Naoya Kaji |date=1984-11-12 |page=135 |isbn=4-473-00885-1}} In [[Jogan]] 11 (869 AD), Emperor [[Seiwa]] ordered the construction of the shrine, enshrining [[Gozu Tenno]] (identified with [[Susanoo]]) from [[Hiromine Shrine]] in [[Harima Province]]. At the time, it served as the guardian shrine of [[Tokoji Temple]], which was located in the same area. In [[Jisho]] 2 (1178 AD), Emperor [[Takakura]] dedicated offerings for the safe childbirth of his co]]. In [[Gen'ō]] 1 (1319 AD), Emperor [[Go-Daigo]] rebuilt the shrine and granted it the Rank of Shoichii (Senior First Rank) along with sacred treasures. During the [[Ōnin War]], the temple was destroyed by fire, leaving only the shrine intact.{{Cite book|title=Showa Kyoto Guidebook |publisher=Obuchi Kaoru |date=1981-10-30 |page=179}} During the [[Keichō era]] (1596–1615), the shrine was renamed to its current name. In the [[Meiji era]], it was designated as a [[Gōsha|District Shrine]]. On [[September 21, 1934]], the [[Muroto Typhoon]] caused strong winds that collapsed the Haiden (worship hall).Many were trapped under collapsed school buildings in Kyoto. ''Osaka Mainichi Shimbun'' (September 21, 1934). ''Showa News Encyclopedia, Vol. 4 (Showa 8–9)''. == Annual Festivals== A signboard at the entrance lists the annual festivals as follows: * [[January 1]] - New Year's Festival (Saitan-sai) * [[January 16]] - Miyashige Inari Festival / Senbo Festival * [[February 3]] - Setsubun Festival * [[March 16]] - Kinensai (Prayer Festival) * [[March]] (Spring Equinox) - Spring Ancestral Festival * [[May 16]] - Senbo Festival * [[June 30]] - Nagoshi-no-Harae (Summer Purification) * [[July 16]] - Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * [[September 16]] - Senbo Festival * [[September]] (Autumn Equinox) - Autumn Ancestral Festival * [[October 16]] - Parishioners' Festival ** Includes a children's procession, children's portable shrine (mikoshi), and hot-water purification ritual (Yugama). Also known as the Okazaki Tenno Festival, Okazaki Festival, or Grand Festival. Historically, there were more than ten large decorative floats (hoko), but they no longer participate in the festival.{{Cite book|author=Takashi Sawa, et al. |title=Kyoto Encyclopedia |publisher=Tankosha |year=1984 |page=135 |isbn=4-473-00885-1}}{{Cite book|author=Itsuo Munemasa, Katsuhisa Moriya |title=Kyoto Saijiki |publisher=Tankosha |year=1986 |page=262 |isbn=4-473-00940-8}} * [[November 16]] - Fire Ritual Festival (Hitaki-sai) * [[November 23]] - Niiname-sai (Harvest Festival) * [[December 2]] - Ame-no-Yashiro Festival * [[December 31]] - Toshikoshi-no-Harae (Year-End Purification) == Enshrined Deities== * Main Deities - [[Susanoo|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Kushinadahime]], [[Gonansanjoshin|Three Female and Five Male Deities]] == Shrine Grounds== * Main Hall * Worship Hall * Ame-no-Yashiro - Deities enshrined: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto]], Kukuno-no-Mikoto, [[Kunisatsuchi|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto]], [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto]], [[Mizuhanome|Mizuhanome-no-Mikoto]] * Shrine Office * Miyashige [[Inari Shrine]] - Deities enshrined: [[Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ebisu|Hiruko-no-Ōkami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Ōkami]] File:Main shrine of Okazaki-jinja.jpg|Many visitors come during the Year of the Rabbit (2023) File:岡崎神社 - 入口石鳥居.jpg|Stone torii at the entrance File:岡崎神社 - 手水舎.JPG|Chozuya (water purification basin) File:岡崎神社 - 宮繁稲荷神社.JPG|Miyashige Inari Shrine File:岡崎神社 - 狛うさぎ1.JPG|Koma-usagi (guardian rabbits) File:Rabbit statue of Okazaki-jinja 001.jpg|Child-granting rabbit statue == Access== === Bus Routes=== * [[Kyoto City Bus]] ''' "Okazaki Shrine-mae" Bus Stop (Routes 32, 93, 203, 204) {{Cite web|url=https://www2.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kotsu/busdia/hyperdia/menu463.htm |title=Okazaki Shrine-mae |publisher=Kyoto City Office |accessdate=2023-03-22}} === Nearest Train Stations=== * [[Kyoto Municipal Subway Tōzai Line]] - [[Keage Station]] * [[Keihan Electric Railway]] - [[Jingū-Marutamachi Station]] {{Shinto navigation}} [[snOkazaki Shrine]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:t, [[Taira no Tokuko]] [[Category:ted Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Okazaki Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Text replacement - "completion tier" to "Completion tier]] }} [[ja:%E5%B2%A1%E5%B4%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Okazaki Shrine]] [[en:Okazaki Shrine]]

{{otheruses|Shinto shrine in Kyoto, Japan|Shinto shrine in Hiroshima, Japan|Okazaki Shrine (Hiroshima)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |Name = Okazaki Shrine |image = [[File:Okazaki-jinja haiden.jpg|260px]]
Haiden |Location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Sakyo Ward]], [[Okazaki (Kyoto)|Okazaki]] Higashitenno-cho 51
{{Cite web|url=http://www.okazakijinja.jp/access/|title=Access|publisher=Okazaki Shrine|accessdate=2017-01-25|ref=}} |Coordinates={{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point|frame-width=200}} |Deity = [[Susanoo|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]]
[[Gonansanjoshin|Three Female and Five Male Deities]] |Shintai = |Shrine Rank = Former [[Gōsha|District Shrine]] |Established = [[Enryaku]] 13 (794 AD) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Other names = Higashitenno-sha, Higashitenno |Pilgrimage = [[Kyoto Jurokushajin]]
[[Zenkoku Seiseikai]] (59 Shrines Enshrining Susanoo-no-Mikoto) |Annual festival = [[July 16]] (Summer Purification Festival) |Rituals = |Map = {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point|frame-width=200}} }} {{osm box|w|1155362848}} '''Okazaki Shrine''' (岡崎神社, Okazaki Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Okazaki (Kyoto)|Okazaki Higashitenno-cho]], [[Sakyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]]. It is also known as "Higashitenno." Since the surrounding area was once inhabited by [[wild rabbits]],{{Cite news|title=Full of rabbits – overcoming hardships with leaps and bounds |newspaper=Asahi Shimbun |page=31 |date=2023-01-16 |author=}} rabbits are considered the shrine’s [[Ujigami|guardian spirits]] and [[shinshi|divine messengers]]. == Overview== Besides being known as a deity of safe childbirth, the shrine is also believed to grant fertility, as rabbits are known for their prolific reproduction. Additionally, its enshrined deities, [[Susanoo|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Kushinadahime]], are associated with having many children. The shrine is also regarded as a place of [[katatagae|directional protection]] due to its historical origins. Several rabbit stone statues can be found throughout the precincts. == History== In [[Enryaku]] 13 (794 AD), during Emperor [[Kanmu]]’s relocation of the capital to [[Heian-kyō]], the shrine was established as one of the [[Taishogun-sha]] (Great General Shrines) built at the four corners of the city to protect the capital. Since it was located to the east, it became known as "Higashitenno."{{Cite book|author= |title=Kyoto Encyclopedia |publisher=Naoya Kaji |date=1984-11-12 |page=135 |isbn=4-473-00885-1}} In [[Jogan]] 11 (869 AD), Emperor [[Seiwa]] ordered the construction of the shrine, enshrining [[Gozu Tenno]] (identified with [[Susanoo]]) from [[Hiromine Shrine]] in [[Harima Province]]. At the time, it served as the guardian shrine of [[Tokoji Temple]], which was located in the same area. In [[Jisho]] 2 (1178 AD), Emperor [[Takakura]] dedicated offerings for the safe childbirth of his co]]. In [[Gen'ō]] 1 (1319 AD), Emperor [[Go-Daigo]] rebuilt the shrine and granted it the Rank of Shoichii (Senior First Rank) along with sacred treasures. During the [[Ōnin War]], the temple was destroyed by fire, leaving only the shrine intact.{{Cite book|title=Showa Kyoto Guidebook |publisher=Obuchi Kaoru |date=1981-10-30 |page=179}} During the [[Keichō era]] (1596–1615), the shrine was renamed to its current name. In the [[Meiji era]], it was designated as a [[Gōsha|District Shrine]]. On [[September 21, 1934]], the [[Muroto Typhoon]] caused strong winds that collapsed the Haiden (worship hall).Many were trapped under collapsed school buildings in Kyoto. ''Osaka Mainichi Shimbun'' (September 21, 1934). ''Showa News Encyclopedia, Vol. 4 (Showa 8–9)''. == Annual Festivals== A signboard at the entrance lists the annual festivals as follows: * [[January 1]] - New Year's Festival (Saitan-sai) * [[January 16]] - Miyashige Inari Festival / Senbo Festival * [[February 3]] - Setsubun Festival * [[March 16]] - Kinensai (Prayer Festival) * [[March]] (Spring Equinox) - Spring Ancestral Festival * [[May 16]] - Senbo Festival * [[June 30]] - Nagoshi-no-Harae (Summer Purification) * [[July 16]] - Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * [[September 16]] - Senbo Festival * [[September]] (Autumn Equinox) - Autumn Ancestral Festival * [[October 16]] - Parishioners' Festival ** Includes a children's procession, children's portable shrine (mikoshi), and hot-water purification ritual (Yugama). Also known as the Okazaki Tenno Festival, Okazaki Festival, or Grand Festival. Historically, there were more than ten large decorative floats (hoko), but they no longer participate in the festival.{{Cite book|author=Takashi Sawa, et al. |title=Kyoto Encyclopedia |publisher=Tankosha |year=1984 |page=135 |isbn=4-473-00885-1}}{{Cite book|author=Itsuo Munemasa, Katsuhisa Moriya |title=Kyoto Saijiki |publisher=Tankosha |year=1986 |page=262 |isbn=4-473-00940-8}} * [[November 16]] - Fire Ritual Festival (Hitaki-sai) * [[November 23]] - Niiname-sai (Harvest Festival) * [[December 2]] - Ame-no-Yashiro Festival * [[December 31]] - Toshikoshi-no-Harae (Year-End Purification) == Enshrined Deities== * Main Deities - [[Susanoo|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Kushinadahime]], [[Gonansanjoshin|Three Female and Five Male Deities]] == Shrine Grounds== * Main Hall * Worship Hall * Ame-no-Yashiro - Deities enshrined: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto]], Kukuno-no-Mikoto, [[Kunisatsuchi|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto]], [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto]], [[Mizuhanome|Mizuhanome-no-Mikoto]] * Shrine Office * Miyashige [[Inari Shrine]] - Deities enshrined: [[Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ebisu|Hiruko-no-Ōkami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Ōkami]] File:Main shrine of Okazaki-jinja.jpg|Many visitors come during the Year of the Rabbit (2023) File:岡崎神社 - 入口石鳥居.jpg|Stone torii at the entrance File:岡崎神社 - 手水舎.JPG|Chozuya (water purification basin) File:岡崎神社 - 宮繁稲荷神社.JPG|Miyashige Inari Shrine File:岡崎神社 - 狛うさぎ1.JPG|Koma-usagi (guardian rabbits) File:Rabbit statue of Okazaki-jinja 001.jpg|Child-granting rabbit statue == Access== === Bus Routes=== * [[Kyoto City Bus]] ''' "Okazaki Shrine-mae" Bus Stop (Routes 32, 93, 203, 204) {{Cite web|url=https://www2.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kotsu/busdia/hyperdia/menu463.htm |title=Okazaki Shrine-mae |publisher=Kyoto City Office |accessdate=2023-03-22}} === Nearest Train Stations=== * [[Kyoto Municipal Subway Tōzai Line]] - [[Keage Station]] * [[Keihan Electric Railway]] - [[Jingū-Marutamachi Station]] {{Shinto navigation}} [[snOkazaki Shrine]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:t, [[Taira no Tokuko]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Okazaki Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Text replacement - "completion tier" to "Completion tier]] }} [[ja:%E5%B2%A1%E5%B4%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Okazaki Shrine]] [[en:Okazaki Shrine]]

''Osaka Mainichi Shimbun'' (September 21, 1934). ''Showa News Encyclopedia, Vol. 4 (Showa 8–9)''. == Annual Festivals== A signboard at the entrance lists the annual festivals as follows: * [[January 1]] - New Year's Festival (Saitan-sai) * [[January 16]] - Miyashige Inari Festival / Senbo Festival * [[February 3]] - Setsubun Festival * [[March 16]] - Kinensai (Prayer Festival) * [[March]] (Spring Equinox) - Spring Ancestral Festival * [[May 16]] - Senbo Festival * [[June 30]] - Nagoshi-no-Harae (Summer Purification) * [[July 16]] - Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * [[September 16]] - Senbo Festival * [[September]] (Autumn Equinox) - Autumn Ancestral Festival * [[October 16]] - Parishioners' Festival ** Includes a children's procession, children's portable shrine (mikoshi), and hot-water purification ritual (Yugama). revision.comment: 60 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:岡崎神社 up to rev 98033517
revision.text:
{{otheruses|京都府京都市にある神社|広島県広島市にある神社|岡崎神社 (広島市)}} {{神社 |名称 = 岡崎神社 |画像 = [[File:Okazaki-jinja haiden.jpg|260px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = [[京都府]][[京都市]][[左京区]][[岡崎 (京都市)|岡崎]]東天王町51
{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.okazakijinja.jp/access/|title=アクセス|publisher=岡﨑神社|accessdate=2017-01-25|ref=}} |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|1|2.7|N|135|47|19.6|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=岡崎神社}} |祭神 = [[スサノオ|速素盞鳴尊]]
[[クシナダヒメ|奇稲田姫命]]
[[五男三女神|三女五男八柱御子神]] |神体 = |社格 = 旧[[郷社]] |創建 = [[延暦]]13年（[[794年]]） |宮司 = |本殿 = |別名 = 東天王社、東天王 |札所等 = [[京都十六社]]
[[全国清々会]]（素戔嗚尊奉祀神社五十九社霊場） |例祭 = [[7月16日]]（お涼み） |神事 = |地図 = {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point|frame-width=200}} }} {{osm box|w|1155362848}} '''岡崎神社'''（おかざきじんじゃ）は、[[京都市]][[左京区]][[岡崎 (京都市)|岡崎]]東天王町にある[[神社]]。別名「東天王」ともいわれる。かつて付近一帯が[[野兎]]の生息地であったことから{{Cite news|title=ウサギづくし　苦難もピョン |newspaper=朝日新聞 |page=31 |date=2023-01-16 |author=}}、兎が[[氏神]]様の[[神使]]とされる。 == 概要 == 安産の神とされる他、多産である兎が神使であること、祭神の速素盞鳴尊と奇稲田姫命に子供が多いことから子授けの神ともされ、神社創建の由来から[[方違え|方除け]]の神ともされている。また、境内のあちらこちらにウサギの石像が存在する。 == 歴史 == [[延暦]]13年（[[794年]]）、[[桓武天皇]]の[[平安京]]遷都の際に、王城鎮護のため平安京の四方に祀った[[大将軍社]]の一つとされ、都の東に鎮座するころから東天王と称される{{Cite book|和書|author= |title=京都大事典 |publisher=納屋嘉治 |date=19841112 |page=135 |isbn=4-473-00885-1}}。 [[清和天皇]]が、[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]11年（[[869年]]）に造営し、[[播磨国]][[広峯神社|廣峯]]から祭神・[[牛頭天王]]（[[スサノオ|速素盞鳴尊]]）を迎え祀り、当時同所にあった東光寺の[[鎮守社]]の役割を果たした。 [[治承]]2年（[[1178年]]）には[[高倉天皇]]の[[中宮]][[平徳子|徳子]]の御産の幣帛を賜ったことにより、安産の神として信仰される。[[元応]]元年（[[1319年]]）には[[後醍醐天皇]]により社殿を再建、正一位の神階と神宝を賜っている。 [[応仁の乱]]の戦火に遭い、寺は焼失し、神社のみが残る{{Cite book|和書|author= |title=昭和京都名所圖會 |publisher=大淵馨 |date=19811030 |page=179 |isbn=}}。 [[慶長]]年間に現在の社名に改称された。 [[明治]]時代に[[郷社]]に列せられている。 [[1934年]]（昭和9年）[[9月21日]]、[[室戸台風]]接近による強風のため拝殿が倒壊京都でも多数が校舎の下敷きに『大阪毎日新聞』昭和9年9月21日号外（『昭和ニュース事典第4巻 昭和8年-昭和9年』本編p228 昭和ニュース事典編纂委員会 毎日コミュニケーションズ刊 1994年）。 == 祭儀 == 入口にある看板には年中祭儀が以下の日程で記載している。 *[[1月1日]] - 歳旦祭 *[[1月16日]] - 宮繁稲荷祭・千度祭 *[[2月3日]] - 節分祭 *[[3月16日]] - 祈年祭 *[[3月]][[春分の日]] - 春季祖霊祭 *[[5月16日]] - 千度祭 *[[6月30日]] - 夏越大祓 *[[7月16日]] - 例大祭 *[[9月16日]] - 千度祭 *[[9月]][[秋分の日]] - 秋季祖霊祭 *[[10月16日]] - 氏子大祭 **稚児行列、子供神輿、湯神楽が催行される。岡崎天王祭、岡崎祭、例大祭などとも称される。 かつては、犬鷹鉾など10基余りの鉾が巡行していたが、現在は巡行していない。{{Cite book|和書 |author=佐和 隆研／[ほか] |title=京都大事典 |publisher=淡交社 |location= |year=1984 |page=135 |isbn=4-473-00885-1 |quote= |date=1984-11-12}}{{Cite book|和書 |author=宗政 五十緒・森谷 尅久／編集 |title=京都歳時記 |publisher=淡交社 |location= |year=1986 |page=262 |isbn=4-473-00940-8 |quote= |date=1986-02-19}} *[[11月16日]] - 火焚祭 *[[11月23日]] - 新嘗祭 *[[12月2日]] - 雨社祭 *[[12月31日]] - 年越大祓 == 祭神 == * 主祭神 - [[スサノオ|速素盞鳴尊]]、[[クシナダヒメ|奇稲田姫命]]、[[五男三女神|三女五男八柱御子神]] == 境内 == * 本殿 * 拝殿 * 雨社 - 祭神：[[オオヤマツミ|大山祇命]]、句々廼馳命、[[国狭槌尊|国挟槌尊]]、[[トヨウケビメ|豊宇気媛命]]、[[ミヅハノメ|闇象女命]] * 社務所 * 宮繁[[稲荷神社]] - 祭神：[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂神]]、[[えびす|蛭子大神]]、[[大国主]]大神 ファイル:Main shrine of Okazaki-jinja.jpg|兎年は多くの参拝客が訪れる（2023年撮影） ファイル:岡崎神社 - 入口石鳥居.jpg|入口石鳥居 ファイル:岡崎神社 - 手水舎.JPG|手水舎 ファイル:岡崎神社 - 宮繁稲荷神社.JPG|宮繁稲荷神社 ファイル:岡崎神社 - 狛うさぎ1.JPG|狛うさぎ ファイル:Rabbit statue of Okazaki-jinja 001.jpg|子授けうさぎ像 == アクセス == ;路線バス * [[京都市営バス]] ** 「'''岡崎神社前'''」バス停（32・93・203・204系統） {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www2.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kotsu/busdia/hyperdia/menu463.htm |title=岡崎神社前 |publisher=京都市役所 |accessdate=2023-03-22}} ** 「'''東天王寺町'''」バス停（32・93・203・204・5系統） {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www2.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kotsu/busdia/hyperdia/menu107.htm |title=東天王町 |publisher=京都市役所 |accessdate=2023-03-22}} ;電車の最寄駅 * [[京都市営地下鉄東西線]]「[[蹴上駅]]」 * [[京阪電車]]「[[神宮丸太町駅]]」 == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Okazaki-jinja}} * [[大将軍 (方位神)]] * [[方位神]] * [[八将神]] * [[方違え]] * [[方違神社]] * [[大将軍神社 (京都市北区)]]（西賀茂大将軍神社） * [[大将軍八神社]] * [[八王子神社]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * [http://www.kobe-gionjinjya.com/origin.html 京都白川 東光寺（兵庫区 平野[[祇園神社 (神戸市兵庫区)|祇園神社]]）] == 外部リンク == [https://okazakijinja.jp/ 岡﨑神社公式サイト] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おかさきしんしや}} [[Category:岡崎 (京都市)]] [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:鎮守社]] [[Category:陰陽道]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧郷社]] [[Category:左京区の建築物]] [[Category:左京区の歴史]] {{pref-stub|pref=京都府}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}}

歳旦祭 *[[1月16日]] - 宮繁稲荷祭・千度祭 *[[2月3日]] - 節分祭 *[[3月16日]] - 祈年祭 *[[3月]][[春分の日]] - 春季祖霊祭 *[[5月16日]] - 千度祭 *[[6月30日]] - 夏越大祓 *[[7月16日]] - 例大祭 *[[9月16日]] - 千度祭 *[[9月]][[秋分の日]] - 秋季祖霊祭 *[[10月16日]] - 氏子大祭 **稚児行列、子供神輿、湯神楽が催行される。岡崎天王祭、岡崎祭、例大祭などとも称される。 かつては、犬鷹鉾など10基余りの鉾が巡行していたが、現在は巡行していない。{{Cite book|和書 |author=佐和 隆研／[ほか] |title=京都大事典 |publisher=淡交社 |location= |year=1984 |page=135 |isbn=4-473-00885-1 |quote= |date=1984-11-12}}{{Cite book|和書 |author=宗政 五十緒・森谷 尅久／編集 |title=京都歳時記 |publisher=淡交社 |location= |year=1986 |page=262 |isbn=4-473-00940-8 |quote= |date=1986-02-19}} *[[11月16日]] - 祭神：[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂神]]、[[えびす|蛭子大神]]、[[大国主]]大神 ファイル:Main shrine of Okazaki-jinja.jpg|兎年は多くの参拝客が訪れる（2023年撮影） ファイル:岡崎神社 - 入口石鳥居.jpg|入口石鳥居 ファイル:岡崎神社 - 手水舎.JPG|手水舎 ファイル:岡崎神社 - 宮繁稲荷神社.JPG|宮繁稲荷神社 ファイル:岡崎神社 - 狛うさぎ1.JPG|狛うさぎ ファイル:Rabbit statue of Okazaki-jinja 001.jpg|子授けうさぎ像 == アクセス == ;路線バス * [[京都市営バス]] ** 「'''岡崎神社前'''」バス停（32・93・203・204系統） {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www2.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kotsu/busdia/hyperdia/menu463.htm |title=岡崎神社前 |publisher=京都市役所 |accessdate=2023-03-22}} ** 「'''東天王寺町'''」バス停（32・93・203・204・5系統） {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www2.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kotsu/busdia/hyperdia/menu107.htm |title=東天王町 |publisher=京都市役所 |accessdate=2023-03-22}} ;電車の最寄駅 * [[京都市営地下鉄東西線]]「[[蹴上駅]]」 * [[京阪電車]]「[[神宮丸太町駅]]」 == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Okazaki-jinja}} * [[大将軍 (方位神)]] * [[方位神]] * [[八将神]] * [[方違え]] * [[方違神社]] * [[大将軍神社 (京都市北区)]]（西賀茂大将軍神社） * [[大将軍八神社]] * [[八王子神社]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * [http://www.kobe-gionjinjya.com/origin.html 京都白川 東光寺（兵庫区 平野[[祇園神社 (神戸市兵庫区)|祇園神社]]）] == 外部リンク == [https://okazakijinja.jp/ 岡﨑神社公式サイト] {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おかさきしんしや}} [[Category:岡崎 (京都市)]] [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:鎮守社]] [[Category:陰陽道]] [[Category:祇園神社]] [[Category:京都府の旧郷社]] [[Category:左京区の建築物]] [[Category:左京区の歴史]] {{pref-stub|pref=京都府}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}}

{{otheruses|Shinto shrine in Kyoto, Japan|Shinto shrine in Hiroshima, Japan|Okazaki Shrine (Hiroshima)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |Name = Okazaki Shrine |image = [[File:Okazaki-jinja haiden.jpg|260px]]
Haiden |Location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Sakyo Ward]], [[Okazaki (Kyoto)|Okazaki]] Higashitenno-cho 51
{{Cite web|url=http://www.okazakijinja.jp/access/|title=Access|publisher=Okazaki Shrine|accessdate=2017-01-25|ref=}} |Coordinates={{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point|frame-width=200}} |Deity = [[Susanoo|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]]
[[Gonansanjoshin|Three Female and Five Male Deities]] |Shintai = |Shrine Rank = Former [[Gōsha|District Shrine]] |Established = [[Enryaku]] 13 (794 AD) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Other names = Higashitenno-sha, Higashitenno |Pilgrimage = [[Kyoto Jurokushajin]]
[[Zenkoku Seiseikai]] (59 Shrines Enshrining Susanoo-no-Mikoto) |Annual festival = [[July 16]] (Summer Purification Festival) |Rituals = |Map = {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point|frame-width=200}} }} {{osm box|w|1155362848}} '''Okazaki Shrine''' (岡崎神社, Okazaki Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Okazaki (Kyoto)|Okazaki Higashitenno-cho]], [[Sakyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]]. It is also known as "Higashitenno." Since the surrounding area was once inhabited by [[wild rabbits]],{{Cite news|title=Full of rabbits – overcoming hardships with leaps and bounds |newspaper=Asahi Shimbun |page=31 |date=2023-01-16 |author=}} rabbits are considered the shrine’s [[Ujigami|guardian spirits]] and [[shinshi|divine messengers]]. == Overview== Besides being known as a deity of safe childbirth, the shrine is also believed to grant fertility, as rabbits are known for their prolific reproduction. Additionally, its enshrined deities, [[Susanoo|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Kushinadahime]], are associated with having many children. The shrine is also regarded as a place of [[katatagae|directional protection]] due to its historical origins. Several rabbit stone statues can be found throughout the precincts. == History== In [[Enryaku]] 13 (794 AD), during Emperor [[Kanmu]]’s relocation of the capital to [[Heian-kyō]], the shrine was established as one of the [[Taishogun-sha]] (Great General Shrines) built at the four corners of the city to protect the capital. Since it was located to the east, it became known as "Higashitenno."{{Cite book|author= |title=Kyoto Encyclopedia |publisher=Naoya Kaji |date=1984-11-12 |page=135 |isbn=4-473-00885-1}} In [[Jogan]] 11 (869 AD), Emperor [[Seiwa]] ordered the construction of the shrine, enshrining [[Gozu Tenno]] (identified with [[Susanoo]]) from [[Hiromine Shrine]] in [[Harima Province]]. At the time, it served as the guardian shrine of [[Tokoji Temple]], which was located in the same area. In [[Jisho]] 2 (1178 AD), Emperor [[Takakura]] dedicated offerings for the safe childbirth of his co]]. In [[Gen'ō]] 1 (1319 AD), Emperor [[Go-Daigo]] rebuilt the shrine and granted it the Rank of Shoichii (Senior First Rank) along with sacred treasures. During the [[Ōnin War]], the temple was destroyed by fire, leaving only the shrine intact.{{Cite book|title=Showa Kyoto Guidebook |publisher=Obuchi Kaoru |date=1981-10-30 |page=179}} During the [[Keichō era]] (1596–1615), the shrine was renamed to its current name. In the [[Meiji era]], it was designated as a [[Gōsha|District Shrine]]. On [[September 21, 1934]], the [[Muroto Typhoon]] caused strong winds that collapsed the Haiden (worship hall).Many were trapped under collapsed school buildings in Kyoto. ''Osaka Mainichi Shimbun'' (September 21, 1934). ''Showa News Encyclopedia, Vol. 4 (Showa 8–9)''. == Annual Festivals== A signboard at the entrance lists the annual festivals as follows: * [[January 1]] - New Year's Festival (Saitan-sai) * [[January 16]] - Miyashige Inari Festival / Senbo Festival * [[February 3]] - Setsubun Festival * [[March 16]] - Kinensai (Prayer Festival) * [[March]] (Spring Equinox) - Spring Ancestral Festival * [[May 16]] - Senbo Festival * [[June 30]] - Nagoshi-no-Harae (Summer Purification) * [[July 16]] - Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * [[September 16]] - Senbo Festival * [[September]] (Autumn Equinox) - Autumn Ancestral Festival * [[October 16]] - Parishioners' Festival ** Includes a children's procession, children's portable shrine (mikoshi), and hot-water purification ritual (Yugama). Also known as the Okazaki Tenno Festival, Okazaki Festival, or Grand Festival. Historically, there were more than ten large decorative floats (hoko), but they no longer participate in the festival.{{Cite book|author=Takashi Sawa, et al. |title=Kyoto Encyclopedia |publisher=Tankosha |year=1984 |page=135 |isbn=4-473-00885-1}}{{Cite book|author=Itsuo Munemasa, Katsuhisa Moriya |title=Kyoto Saijiki |publisher=Tankosha |year=1986 |page=262 |isbn=4-473-00940-8}} * [[November 16]] - Fire Ritual Festival (Hitaki-sai) * [[November 23]] - Niiname-sai (Harvest Festival) * [[December 2]] - Ame-no-Yashiro Festival * [[December 31]] - Toshikoshi-no-Harae (Year-End Purification) == Enshrined Deities== * Main Deities - Deities enshrined: [[Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ebisu|Hiruko-no-Ōkami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Ōkami]] File:Main shrine of Okazaki-jinja.jpg|Many visitors come during the Year of the Rabbit (2023) File:岡崎神社 - {{otheruses|Shinto shrine in Kyoto, Japan|Shinto shrine in Hiroshima, Japan|Okazaki Shrine (Hiroshima)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |Name = Okazaki Shrine |image = [[File:Okazaki-jinja haiden.jpg|260px]]
Haiden |Location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[Kyoto City]], [[Sakyo Ward]], [[Okazaki (Kyoto)|Okazaki]] Higashitenno-cho 51
{{Cite web|url=http://www.okazakijinja.jp/access/|title=Access|publisher=Okazaki Shrine|accessdate=2017-01-25|ref=}} |Coordinates={{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point|frame-width=200}} |Deity = [[Susanoo|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]]
[[Gonansanjoshin|Three Female and Five Male Deities]] |Shintai = |Shrine Rank = Former [[Gōsha|District Shrine]] |Established = [[Enryaku]] 13 (794 AD) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Other names = Higashitenno-sha, Higashitenno |Pilgrimage = [[Kyoto Jurokushajin]]
[[Zenkoku Seiseikai]] (59 Shrines Enshrining Susanoo-no-Mikoto) |Annual festival = [[July 16]] (Summer Purification Festival) |Rituals = |Map = {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point|frame-width=200}} }} {{osm box|w|1155362848}} '''Okazaki Shrine''' (岡崎神社, Okazaki Jinja) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Okazaki (Kyoto)|Okazaki Higashitenno-cho]], [[Sakyo Ward]], [[Kyoto City]]. It is also known as "Higashitenno." Since the surrounding area was once inhabited by [[wild rabbits]],{{Cite news|title=Full of rabbits – overcoming hardships with leaps and bounds |newspaper=Asahi Shimbun |page=31 |date=2023-01-16 |author=}} rabbits are considered the shrine’s [[Ujigami|guardian spirits]] and [[shinshi|divine messengers]]. == Overview== Besides being known as a deity of safe childbirth, the shrine is also believed to grant fertility, as rabbits are known for their prolific reproduction. Additionally, its enshrined deities, [[Susanoo|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Kushinadahime]], are associated with having many children. The shrine is also regarded as a place of [[katatagae|directional protection]] due to its historical origins. Several rabbit stone statues can be found throughout the precincts. == History== In [[Enryaku]] 13 (794 AD), during Emperor [[Kanmu]]’s relocation of the capital to [[Heian-kyō]], the shrine was established as one of the [[Taishogun-sha]] (Great General Shrines) built at the four corners of the city to protect the capital. Since it was located to the east, it became known as "Higashitenno."{{Cite book|author= |title=Kyoto Encyclopedia |publisher=Naoya Kaji |date=1984-11-12 |page=135 |isbn=4-473-00885-1}} In [[Jogan]] 11 (869 AD), Emperor [[Seiwa]] ordered the construction of the shrine, enshrining [[Gozu Tenno]] (identified with [[Susanoo]]) from [[Hiromine Shrine]] in [[Harima Province]]. At the time, it served as the guardian shrine of [[Tokoji Temple]], which was located in the same area. In [[Jisho]] 2 (1178 AD), Emperor [[Takakura]] dedicated offerings for the safe childbirth of his co]]. In [[Gen'ō]] 1 (1319 AD), Emperor [[Go-Daigo]] rebuilt the shrine and granted it the Rank of Shoichii (Senior First Rank) along with sacred treasures. During the [[Ōnin War]], the temple was destroyed by fire, leaving only the shrine intact.{{Cite book|title=Showa Kyoto Guidebook |publisher=Obuchi Kaoru |date=1981-10-30 |page=179}} During the [[Keichō era]] (1596–1615), the shrine was renamed to its current name. In the [[Meiji era]], it was designated as a [[Gōsha|District Shrine]]. On [[September 21, 1934]], the [[Muroto Typhoon]] caused strong winds that collapsed the Haiden (worship hall).Many were trapped under collapsed school buildings in Kyoto. ''Osaka Mainichi Shimbun'' (September 21, 1934). ''Showa News Encyclopedia, Vol. 4 (Showa 8–9)''. == Annual Festivals== A signboard at the entrance lists the annual festivals as follows: * [[January 1]] - New Year's Festival (Saitan-sai) * [[January 16]] - Miyashige Inari Festival / Senbo Festival * [[February 3]] - Setsubun Festival * [[March 16]] - Kinensai (Prayer Festival) * [[March]] (Spring Equinox) - Spring Ancestral Festival * [[May 16]] - Senbo Festival * [[June 30]] - Nagoshi-no-Harae (Summer Purification) * [[July 16]] - Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * [[September 16]] - Senbo Festival * [[September]] (Autumn Equinox) - Autumn Ancestral Festival * [[October 16]] - Parishioners' Festival ** Includes a children's procession, children's portable shrine (mikoshi), and hot-water purification ritual (Yugama). Also known as the Okazaki Tenno Festival, Okazaki Festival, or Grand Festival. Historically, there were more than ten large decorative floats (hoko), but they no longer participate in the festival.{{Cite book|author=Takashi Sawa, et al. |title=Kyoto Encyclopedia |publisher=Tankosha |year=1984 |page=135 |isbn=4-473-00885-1}}{{Cite book|author=Itsuo Munemasa, Katsuhisa Moriya |title=Kyoto Saijiki |publisher=Tankosha |year=1986 |page=262 |isbn=4-473-00940-8}} * [[November 16]] - Fire Ritual Festival (Hitaki-sai) * [[November 23]] - Niiname-sai (Harvest Festival) * [[December 2]] - Ame-no-Yashiro Festival * [[December 31]] - Toshikoshi-no-Harae (Year-End Purification) == Enshrined Deities== * Main Deities - [[Susanoo|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Kushinadahime]], [[Gonansanjoshin|Three Female and Five Male Deities]] == Shrine Grounds== * Main Hall * Worship Hall * Ame-no-Yashiro - Deities enshrined: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto]], Kukuno-no-Mikoto, [[Kunisatsuchi|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto]], [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto]], [[Mizuhanome|Mizuhanome-no-Mikoto]] * Shrine Office * Miyashige [[Inari Shrine]] - Deities enshrined: [[Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ebisu|Hiruko-no-Ōkami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Ōkami]] File:Main shrine of Okazaki-jinja.jpg|Many visitors come during the Year of the Rabbit (2023) File:岡崎神社 - 入口石鳥居.jpg|Stone torii at the entrance File:岡崎神社 - 手水舎.JPG|Chozuya (water purification basin) File:岡崎神社 - 宮繁稲荷神社.JPG|Miyashige Inari Shrine File:岡崎神社 - 狛うさぎ1.JPG|Koma-usagi (guardian rabbits) File:Rabbit statue of Okazaki-jinja 001.jpg|Child-granting rabbit statue == Access== === Bus Routes=== * [[Kyoto City Bus]] ''' "Okazaki Shrine-mae" Bus Stop (Routes 32, 93, 203, 204) {{Cite web|url=https://www2.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kotsu/busdia/hyperdia/menu463.htm |title=Okazaki Shrine-mae |publisher=Kyoto City Office |accessdate=2023-03-22}} === Nearest Train Stations=== * [[Kyoto Municipal Subway Tōzai Line]] - [[Keage Station]] * [[Keihan Electric Railway]] - [[Jingū-Marutamachi Station]] {{Shinto navigation}} [[snOkazaki Shrine]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:t, [[Taira no Tokuko]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Okazaki Shrine]] }} [[ja:%E5%B2%A1%E5%B4%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Okazaki Shrine]] [[en:Okazaki Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|岡崎神社|version=98033517|comment=Imported full ja history}}
