In [[:en:Jisho|Jisho]] 2 (1178 AD), Emperor [[:en:Takakura|Takakura]] dedicated offerings for the safe childbirth of his co]]. In [[:en:Gen'ō|Gen'ō]] 1 (1319 AD), Emperor [[:en:Go-Daigo|Go-Daigo]] rebuilt the shrine and granted it the Rank of Shoichii (Senior First Rank) along with sacred treasures. During the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], the temple was destroyed by fire, leaving only the shrine intact.{{Cite book|title=Showa Kyoto Guidebook |publisher=Obuchi Kaoru |date=1981-10-30 |page=179}} During the [[:en:Keichō era|Keichō era]] (1596–1615), the shrine was renamed to its current name. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was designated as a [[Gōsha|District Shrine]]. On [[September 21, 1934]], the [[:en:Muroto Typhoon|Muroto Typhoon]] caused strong winds that collapsed the Haiden (worship hall).Many were trapped under collapsed school buildings in Kyoto. ''Osaka Mainichi Shimbun'' (September 21, 1934). ''Showa News Encyclopedia, Vol. 4 (Showa 8–9)''. == Annual Festivals== A signboard at the entrance lists the annual festivals as follows: * [[:en:January 1|January 1]] - New Year's Festival (Saitan-sai) * [[:en:January 16|January 16]] - Miyashige Inari Festival / Senbo Festival * [[:en:February 3|February 3]] - Setsubun Festival * [[:en:March 16|March 16]] - Kinensai (Prayer Festival) * [[:en:March|March]] (Spring Equinox) - Spring Ancestral Festival * [[:en:May 16|May 16]] - Senbo Festival * [[:en:June 30|June 30]] - Nagoshi-no-Harae (Summer Purification) * [[:en:July 16|July 16]] - Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * [[:en:September 16|September 16]] - Senbo Festival * [[:en:September|September]] (Autumn Equinox) - Autumn Ancestral Festival * [[:en:October 16|October 16]] - Parishioners' Festival ** Includes a children's procession, children's portable shrine (mikoshi), and hot-water purification ritual (Yugama). Also known as the Okazaki Tenno Festival, Okazaki Festival, or Grand Festival. Historically, there were more than ten large decorative floats (hoko), but they no longer participate in the festival.{{Cite book|author=Takashi Sawa, et al. |title=Kyoto Encyclopedia |publisher=Tankosha |year=1984 |page=135 |isbn=4-473-00885-1}}{{Cite book|author=Itsuo Munemasa, Katsuhisa Moriya |title=Kyoto Saijiki |publisher=Tankosha |year=1986 |page=262 |isbn=4-473-00940-8}} * [[:en:November 16|November 16]] - Fire Ritual Festival (Hitaki-sai) * [[:en:November 23|November 23]] - Niiname-sai (Harvest Festival) * [[:en:December 2|December 2]] - Ame-no-Yashiro Festival * [[:en:December 31|December 31]] - Toshikoshi-no-Harae (Year-End Purification) == Enshrined Deities== * Main Deities - [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Kushinadahime]], [[Gonansanjoshin|Three Female and Five Male Deities]] == Shrine Grounds== * Main Hall * Worship Hall * Ame-no-Yashiro - Deities enshrined: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto]], Kukuno-no-Mikoto, [[Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto]], [[Mizuhanome|Mizuhanome-no-Mikoto]] * Shrine Office * Miyashige [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari shrine]] - Deities enshrined: [[Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ebisu|Hiruko-no-Ōkami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Ōkami]] File:Main shrine of Okazaki-jinja.jpg|Many visitors come during the Year of the Rabbit (2023) File:岡崎神社 - 入口石鳥居.jpg|Stone torii at the entrance File:岡崎神社 - 手水舎.JPG|Chozuya (water purification basin) File:岡崎神社 - 宮繁稲荷神社.JPG|Miyashige Inari Shrine File:岡崎神社 - 狛うさぎ1.JPG|Koma-usagi (guardian rabbits) File:Rabbit statue of Okazaki-jinja 001.jpg|Child-granting rabbit statue == Access== === Bus Routes=== * [[Kyoto City Bus]] ''' "Okazaki Shrine-mae" Bus Stop (Routes 32, 93, 203, 204) {{Cite web|url=https://www2.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kotsu/busdia/hyperdia/menu463.htm |title=Okazaki Shrine-mae |publisher=Kyoto City Office |accessdate=2023-03-22}} === Nearest Train Stations=== * [[Kyoto Municipal Subway Tōzai Line]] - [[:en:Keage Station|Keage Station]] * [[:en:Keihan Electric Railway|Keihan Electric Railway]] - [[:en:Jingū-Marutamachi Station|Jingū-Marutamachi Station]] {{Shinto navigation}} [[snOkazaki Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|岡崎神社|version=98033517|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Okazaki-jinja}} [[ja:岡崎神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11472971}} [[Category:Buildings in Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:History of Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Okazaki, Sakyō-ku]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:t, [[Taira no Tokuko]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Okazaki-jinja]][[ja:岡崎神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Okazaki-jinja]][[ja:岡崎神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Okazaki-jinja]][[ja:岡崎神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Okazaki-jinja haiden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Sakyō-ku|WD=Q1321832}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.017417|N|135.788778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11472971]] Wikidata name: Okazaki Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses|Shinto shrine in Kyoto, Japan|Shinto shrine in Hiroshima, Japan|Okazaki Shrine (Hiroshima)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |Name = Okazaki Shrine |image = [[File:Okazaki-jinja haiden.jpg|260px]]
Haiden |Location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]], [[Sakyo Ward]], [[Okazaki (Kyoto)|Okazaki]] Higashitenno-cho 51
{{Cite web|url=http://www.okazakijinja.jp/access/|title=Access|publisher=Okazaki Shrine|accessdate=2017-01-25|ref=}} |Coordinates={{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point|frame-width=200}} |Deity = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]]
[[Gonansanjoshin|Three Female and Five Male Deities]] |Shintai = |Shrine Rank = Former [[Gōsha|District Shrine]] |Established = [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] 13 (794 AD) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Other names = Higashitenno-sha, Higashitenno |Pilgrimage = [[Kyoto Jurokushajin]]
[[Zenkoku Seiseikai]] (59 Shrines Enshrining Susanoo-no-Mikoto) |Annual festival = [[:en:July 16|July 16]] (Summer Purification Festival) |Rituals = |Map = {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point|frame-width=200}} }} {{osm box|w|1155362848}} '''Okazaki Shrine''' (岡崎神社, Okazaki Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Okazaki (Kyoto)|Okazaki Higashitenno-cho]], [[Sakyo Ward]], [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]]. It is also known as "Higashitenno." Since the surrounding area was once inhabited by [[:en:wild rabbits|wild rabbits]],{{Cite news|title=Full of rabbits – overcoming hardships with leaps and bounds |newspaper=Asahi Shimbun |page=31 |date=2023-01-16 |author=}} rabbits are considered the shrine’s [[Ujigami|guardian spirits]] and [[:en:Shinshi|divine messengers]]. == Overview== Besides being known as a deity of safe childbirth, the shrine is also believed to grant fertility, as rabbits are known for their prolific reproduction. Additionally, its enshrined deities, [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Kushinadahime]], are associated with having many children. The shrine is also regarded as a place of [[katatagae|directional protection]] due to its historical origins. Several rabbit stone statues can be found throughout the precincts. == History== In [[:en:Enryaku|Enryaku]] 13 (794 AD), during Emperor [[:en:Kanmu|Kanmu]]’s relocation of the capital to [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyō]], the shrine was established as one of the [[Taishogun-sha]] (Great General Shrines) built at the four corners of the city to protect the capital. Since it was located to the east, it became known as "Higashitenno."{{Cite book|author= |title=Kyoto Encyclopedia |publisher=Naoya Kaji |date=1984-11-12 |page=135 |isbn=4-473-00885-1}} In [[:en:Jogan|Jogan]] 11 (869 AD), Emperor [[:en:Seiwa|Seiwa]] ordered the construction of the shrine, enshrining [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]] (identified with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]) from [[Hiromine Shrine]] in [[:en:Harima Province|Harima Province]]. At the time, it served as the guardian shrine of [[Tokoji Temple]], which was located in the same area. In [[:en:Jisho|Jisho]] 2 (1178 AD), Emperor [[:en:Takakura|Takakura]] dedicated offerings for the safe childbirth of his co]]. In [[:en:Gen'ō|Gen'ō]] 1 (1319 AD), Emperor [[:en:Go-Daigo|Go-Daigo]] rebuilt the shrine and granted it the Rank of Shoichii (Senior First Rank) along with sacred treasures. During the [[:en:Ōnin War|Ōnin War]], the temple was destroyed by fire, leaving only the shrine intact.{{Cite book|title=Showa Kyoto Guidebook |publisher=Obuchi Kaoru |date=1981-10-30 |page=179}} During the [[:en:Keichō era|Keichō era]] (1596–1615), the shrine was renamed to its current name. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], it was designated as a [[Gōsha|District Shrine]]. On [[September 21, 1934]], the [[:en:Muroto Typhoon|Muroto Typhoon]] caused strong winds that collapsed the Haiden (worship hall).Many were trapped under collapsed school buildings in Kyoto. ''Osaka Mainichi Shimbun'' (September 21, 1934). ''Showa News Encyclopedia, Vol. 4 (Showa 8–9)''. == Annual Festivals== A signboard at the entrance lists the annual festivals as follows: * [[:en:January 1|January 1]] - New Year's Festival (Saitan-sai) * [[:en:January 16|January 16]] - Miyashige Inari Festival / Senbo Festival * [[:en:February 3|February 3]] - Setsubun Festival * [[:en:March 16|March 16]] - Kinensai (Prayer Festival) * [[:en:March|March]] (Spring Equinox) - Spring Ancestral Festival * [[:en:May 16|May 16]] - Senbo Festival * [[:en:June 30|June 30]] - Nagoshi-no-Harae (Summer Purification) * [[:en:July 16|July 16]] - Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * [[:en:September 16|September 16]] - Senbo Festival * [[:en:September|September]] (Autumn Equinox) - Autumn Ancestral Festival * [[:en:October 16|October 16]] - Parishioners' Festival ** Includes a children's procession, children's portable shrine (mikoshi), and hot-water purification ritual (Yugama). Also known as the Okazaki Tenno Festival, Okazaki Festival, or Grand Festival. Historically, there were more than ten large decorative floats (hoko), but they no longer participate in the festival.{{Cite book|author=Takashi Sawa, et al. |title=Kyoto Encyclopedia |publisher=Tankosha |year=1984 |page=135 |isbn=4-473-00885-1}}{{Cite book|author=Itsuo Munemasa, Katsuhisa Moriya |title=Kyoto Saijiki |publisher=Tankosha |year=1986 |page=262 |isbn=4-473-00940-8}} * [[:en:November 16|November 16]] - Fire Ritual Festival (Hitaki-sai) * [[:en:November 23|November 23]] - Niiname-sai (Harvest Festival) * [[:en:December 2|December 2]] - Ame-no-Yashiro Festival * [[:en:December 31|December 31]] - Toshikoshi-no-Harae (Year-End Purification) == Enshrined Deities== * Main Deities - [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Kushinadahime]], [[Gonansanjoshin|Three Female and Five Male Deities]] == Shrine Grounds== * Main Hall * Worship Hall * Ame-no-Yashiro - Deities enshrined: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto]], Kukuno-no-Mikoto, [[Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto]], [[Mizuhanome|Mizuhanome-no-Mikoto]] * Shrine Office * Miyashige [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari shrine]] - {{ill|Keage Station|en|Keage Station|WD=Q1004370|lt=Keage Station}} * {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|en|Keihan Electric Railway|WD=Q1188274|lt=Keihan Electric Railway}} - {{ill|Jingū-Marutamachi Station|en|Jingū-Marutamachi Station|WD=Q2244767|lt=Jingū-Marutamachi Station}} {{Shinto navigation}} [[snOkazaki Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|岡崎神社|version=98033517|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Okazaki-jinja}} [[ja:岡崎神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11472971}} [[Category:Buildings in Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:History of Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Okazaki, Sakyō-ku]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:t, [[Taira no Tokuko]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Okazaki-jinja]][[ja:岡崎神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Okazaki-jinja]][[ja:岡崎神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Okazaki-jinja]][[ja:岡崎神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Okazaki-jinja haiden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Sakyō-ku|WD=Q1321832}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.017417|N|135.788778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11472971]] Wikidata name: Okazaki Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{otheruses|Shinto shrine in Kyoto, Japan|Shinto shrine in Hiroshima, Japan|Okazaki Shrine (Hiroshima)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |Name = Okazaki Shrine |image = [[File:Okazaki-jinja haiden.jpg|260px]]
Haiden |Location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]], [[Sakyo Ward]], [[Okazaki (Kyoto)|Okazaki]] Higashitenno-cho 51
{{Cite web|url=http://www.okazakijinja.jp/access/|title=Access|publisher=Okazaki Shrine|accessdate=2017-01-25|ref=}} |Coordinates={{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point|frame-width=200}} |Deity = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]]
[[Gonansanjoshin|Three Female and Five Male Deities]] |Shintai = |Shrine Rank = Former [[Gōsha|District Shrine]] |Established = {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku}} 13 (794 AD) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Other names = Higashitenno-sha, Higashitenno |Pilgrimage = [[Kyoto Jurokushajin]]
[[Zenkoku Seiseikai]] (59 Shrines Enshrining Susanoo-no-Mikoto) |Annual festival = {{ill|July 16|en|July 16|WD=Q2731|lt=July 16}} (Summer Purification Festival) |Rituals = |Map = {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point|frame-width=200}} }} {{osm box|w|1155362848}} '''Okazaki Shrine''' (岡崎神社, Okazaki Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Okazaki (Kyoto)|Okazaki Higashitenno-cho]], [[Sakyo Ward]], [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]]. It is also known as "Higashitenno." Since the surrounding area was once inhabited by [[:en:wild rabbits|wild rabbits]],{{Cite news|title=Full of rabbits – overcoming hardships with leaps and bounds |newspaper=Asahi Shimbun |page=31 |date=2023-01-16 |author=}} rabbits are considered the shrine’s [[Ujigami|guardian spirits]] and {{ill|Shinshi|en|Shinshi|WD=Q10444973|lt=divine messengers}}. == Overview== Besides being known as a deity of safe childbirth, the shrine is also believed to grant fertility, as rabbits are known for their prolific reproduction. Additionally, its enshrined deities, [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Kushinadahime]], are associated with having many children. The shrine is also regarded as a place of [[katatagae|directional protection]] due to its historical origins. Several rabbit stone statues can be found throughout the precincts. == History== In {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku}} 13 (794 AD), during Emperor [[:en:Kanmu|Kanmu]]’s relocation of the capital to {{ill|Heian-kyō|en|Heian-kyō|WD=Q751907|lt=Heian-kyō}}, the shrine was established as one of the [[Taishogun-sha]] (Great General Shrines) built at the four corners of the city to protect the capital. Since it was located to the east, it became known as "Higashitenno."{{Cite book|author= |title=Kyoto Encyclopedia |publisher=Naoya Kaji |date=1984-11-12 |page=135 |isbn=4-473-00885-1}} In {{ill|Jogan|en|Jogan|WD=Q6214690|lt=Jogan}} 11 (869 AD), Emperor {{ill|Seiwa|en|Seiwa|WD=Q16871613|lt=Seiwa}} ordered the construction of the shrine, enshrining [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]] (identified with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]) from [[Hiromine Shrine]] in {{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|WD=Q1047523|lt=Harima Province}}. At the time, it served as the guardian shrine of [[Tokoji Temple]], which was located in the same area. In {{ill|Jisho|en|Jisho|WD=Q114978004|lt=Jisho}} 2 (1178 AD), Emperor {{ill|Takakura|en|Takakura|WD=Q7677112|lt=Takakura}} dedicated offerings for the safe childbirth of his co]]. In {{ill|Gen'ō|en|Gen'ō|WD=Q1193925|lt=Gen'ō}} 1 (1319 AD), Emperor [[:en:Go-Daigo|Go-Daigo]] rebuilt the shrine and granted it the Rank of Shoichii (Senior First Rank) along with sacred treasures. During the {{ill|Ōnin War|en|Ōnin War|WD=Q385676|lt=Ōnin War}}, the temple was destroyed by fire, leaving only the shrine intact.{{Cite book|title=Showa Kyoto Guidebook |publisher=Obuchi Kaoru |date=1981-10-30 |page=179}} During the [[:en:Keichō era|Keichō era]] (1596–1615), the shrine was renamed to its current name. In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, it was designated as a [[Gōsha|District Shrine]]. On [[September 21, 1934]], the [[:en:Muroto Typhoon|Muroto Typhoon]] caused strong winds that collapsed the Haiden (worship hall).Many were trapped under collapsed school buildings in Kyoto. ''Osaka Mainichi Shimbun'' (September 21, 1934). ''Showa News Encyclopedia, Vol. 4 (Showa 8–9)''. == Annual Festivals== A signboard at the entrance lists the annual festivals as follows: * {{ill|January 1|en|January 1|WD=Q2150|lt=January 1}} - New Year's Festival (Saitan-sai) * {{ill|January 16|en|January 16|WD=Q2261|lt=January 16}} - Miyashige Inari Festival / Senbo Festival * {{ill|February 3|en|February 3|WD=Q2315|lt=February 3}} - Setsubun Festival * {{ill|March 16|en|March 16|WD=Q2417|lt=March 16}} - Kinensai (Prayer Festival) * {{ill|March|en|March|WD=Q110|lt=March}} (Spring Equinox) - Spring Ancestral Festival * {{ill|May 16|en|May 16|WD=Q2574|lt=May 16}} - Senbo Festival * {{ill|June 30|en|June 30|WD=Q2657|lt=June 30}} - Nagoshi-no-Harae (Summer Purification) * {{ill|July 16|en|July 16|WD=Q2731|lt=July 16}} - Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * {{ill|September 16|en|September 16|WD=Q2897|lt=September 16}} - Senbo Festival * {{ill|September|en|September|WD=Q123|lt=September}} (Autumn Equinox) - Autumn Ancestral Festival * {{ill|October 16|en|October 16|WD=Q2963|lt=October 16}} - Parishioners' Festival ** Includes a children's procession, children's portable shrine (mikoshi), and hot-water purification ritual (Yugama). Also known as the Okazaki Tenno Festival, Okazaki Festival, or Grand Festival. Historically, there were more than ten large decorative floats (hoko), but they no longer participate in the festival.{{Cite book|author=Takashi Sawa, et al. |title=Kyoto Encyclopedia |publisher=Tankosha |year=1984 |page=135 |isbn=4-473-00885-1}}{{Cite book|author=Itsuo Munemasa, Katsuhisa Moriya |title=Kyoto Saijiki |publisher=Tankosha |year=1986 |page=262 |isbn=4-473-00940-8}} * {{ill|November 16|en|November 16|WD=Q3029|lt=November 16}} - Fire Ritual Festival (Hitaki-sai) * {{ill|November 23|en|November 23|WD=Q3021|lt=November 23}} - Niiname-sai (Harvest Festival) * {{ill|December 2|en|December 2|WD=Q2296|lt=December 2}} - Ame-no-Yashiro Festival * {{ill|December 31|en|December 31|WD=Q2912|lt=December 31}} - Toshikoshi-no-Harae (Year-End Purification) == Enshrined Deities== * Main Deities - [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Kushinadahime]], [[Gonansanjoshin|Three Female and Five Male Deities]] == Shrine Grounds== * Main Hall * Worship Hall * Ame-no-Yashiro - Deities enshrined: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto]], Kukuno-no-Mikoto, [[Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto]], {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto}}, [[Mizuhanome|Mizuhanome-no-Mikoto]] * Shrine Office * Miyashige {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari shrine}} - Deities enshrined: [[Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ebisu|Hiruko-no-Ōkami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Ōkami]] File:Main shrine of Okazaki-jinja.jpg|Many visitors come during the Year of the Rabbit (2023) File:岡崎神社 - 入口石鳥居.jpg|Stone torii at the entrance File:岡崎神社 - 手水舎.JPG|Chozuya (water purification basin) File:岡崎神社 - 宮繁稲荷神社.JPG|Miyashige Inari Shrine File:岡崎神社 - 狛うさぎ1.JPG|Koma-usagi (guardian rabbits) File:Rabbit statue of Okazaki-jinja 001.jpg|Child-granting rabbit statue == Access== === Bus Routes=== * [[Kyoto City Bus]] ''' "Okazaki Shrine-mae" Bus Stop (Routes 32, 93, 203, 204) {{Cite web|url=https://www2.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kotsu/busdia/hyperdia/menu463.htm |title=Okazaki Shrine-mae |publisher=Kyoto City Office |accessdate=2023-03-22}} === Nearest Train Stations=== * [[Kyoto Municipal Subway Tōzai Line]] - {{ill|Keage Station|en|Keage Station|WD=Q1004370|lt=Keage Station}} * {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|en|Keihan Electric Railway|WD=Q1188274|lt=Keihan Electric Railway}} - {{ill|Jingū-Marutamachi Station|en|Jingū-Marutamachi Station|WD=Q2244767|lt=Jingū-Marutamachi Station}} {{Shinto navigation}} [[snOkazaki Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|岡崎神社|version=98033517|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Okazaki-jinja}} [[ja:岡崎神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11472971}} [[Category:Buildings in Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:History of Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Okazaki, Sakyō-ku]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:t, [[Taira no Tokuko]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]]
[[Gonansanjoshin|Three Female and Five Male Deities]] |Shintai = |Shrine Rank = Former [[Gōsha|District Shrine]] |Established = {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku}} 13 (794 AD) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Other names = Higashitenno-sha, Higashitenno |Pilgrimage = [[Kyoto Jurokushajin]]
[[Zenkoku Seiseikai]] (59 Shrines Enshrining Susanoo-no-Mikoto) |Annual festival = {{ill|July 16|en|July 16|WD=Q2731|lt=July 16}} (Summer Purification Festival) |Rituals = |Map = {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point|frame-width=200}} }} {{osm box|w|1155362848}} '''Okazaki Shrine''' (岡崎神社, Okazaki Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Okazaki (Kyoto)|Okazaki Higashitenno-cho]], [[Sakyo Ward]], [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]]. It is also known as "Higashitenno." Since the surrounding area was once inhabited by [[:en:wild rabbits|wild rabbits]],{{Cite news|title=Full of rabbits – overcoming hardships with leaps and bounds |newspaper=Asahi Shimbun |page=31 |date=2023-01-16 |author=}} rabbits are considered the shrine’s [[Ujigami|guardian spirits]] and {{ill|Shinshi|en|Shinshi|WD=Q10444973|lt=divine messengers}}. == Overview== Besides being known as a deity of safe childbirth, the shrine is also believed to grant fertility, as rabbits are known for their prolific reproduction. Additionally, its enshrined deities, [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Kushinadahime]], are associated with having many children. The shrine is also regarded as a place of [[katatagae|directional protection]] due to its historical origins. Several rabbit stone statues can be found throughout the precincts. == History== In {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku}} 13 (794 AD), during Emperor [[:en:Kanmu|Kanmu]]’s relocation of the capital to {{ill|Heian-kyō|en|Heian-kyō|WD=Q751907|lt=Heian-kyō}}, the shrine was established as one of the [[Taishogun-sha]] (Great General Shrines) built at the four corners of the city to protect the capital. Since it was located to the east, it became known as "Higashitenno."{{Cite book|author= |title=Kyoto Encyclopedia |publisher=Naoya Kaji |date=1984-11-12 |page=135 |isbn=4-473-00885-1}} In {{ill|Jogan|en|Jogan|WD=Q6214690|lt=Jogan}} 11 (869 AD), Emperor {{ill|Seiwa|en|Seiwa|WD=Q16871613|lt=Seiwa}} ordered the construction of the shrine, enshrining [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]] (identified with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]) from [[Hiromine Shrine]] in {{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|WD=Q1047523|lt=Harima Province}}. At the time, it served as the guardian shrine of [[Tokoji Temple]], which was located in the same area. In {{ill|Jisho|en|Jisho|WD=Q114978004|lt=Jisho}} 2 (1178 AD), Emperor {{ill|Takakura|en|Takakura|WD=Q7677112|lt=Takakura}} dedicated offerings for the safe childbirth of his co]]. In {{ill|Gen'ō|en|Gen'ō|WD=Q1193925|lt=Gen'ō}} 1 (1319 AD), Emperor [[:en:Go-Daigo|Go-Daigo]] rebuilt the shrine and granted it the Rank of Shoichii (Senior First Rank) along with sacred treasures. During the {{ill|Ōnin War|en|Ōnin War|WD=Q385676|lt=Ōnin War}}, the temple was destroyed by fire, leaving only the shrine intact.{{Cite book|title=Showa Kyoto Guidebook |publisher=Obuchi Kaoru |date=1981-10-30 |page=179}} During the [[:en:Keichō era|Keichō era]] (1596–1615), the shrine was renamed to its current name. In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, it was designated as a [[Gōsha|District Shrine]]. On [[September 21, 1934]], the [[:en:Muroto Typhoon|Muroto Typhoon]] caused strong winds that collapsed the Haiden (worship hall).Many were trapped under collapsed school buildings in Kyoto. ''Osaka Mainichi Shimbun'' (September 21, 1934). ''Showa News Encyclopedia, Vol. 4 (Showa 8–9)''. == Annual Festivals== A signboard at the entrance lists the annual festivals as follows: * {{ill|January 1|en|January 1|WD=Q2150|lt=January 1}} - New Year's Festival (Saitan-sai) * {{ill|January 16|en|January 16|WD=Q2261|lt=January 16}} - Miyashige Inari Festival / Senbo Festival * {{ill|February 3|en|February 3|WD=Q2315|lt=February 3}} - Setsubun Festival * {{ill|March 16|en|March 16|WD=Q2417|lt=March 16}} - Kinensai (Prayer Festival) * {{ill|March|en|March|WD=Q110|lt=March}} (Spring Equinox) - Spring Ancestral Festival * {{ill|May 16|en|May 16|WD=Q2574|lt=May 16}} - Senbo Festival * {{ill|June 30|en|June 30|WD=Q2657|lt=June 30}} - Nagoshi-no-Harae (Summer Purification) * {{ill|July 16|en|July 16|WD=Q2731|lt=July 16}} - Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * {{ill|September 16|en|September 16|WD=Q2897|lt=September 16}} - Senbo Festival * {{ill|September|en|September|WD=Q123|lt=September}} (Autumn Equinox) - Autumn Ancestral Festival * {{ill|October 16|en|October 16|WD=Q2963|lt=October 16}} - Parishioners' Festival ** Includes a children's procession, children's portable shrine (mikoshi), and hot-water purification ritual (Yugama). Also known as the Okazaki Tenno Festival, Okazaki Festival, or Grand Festival. Historically, there were more than ten large decorative floats (hoko), but they no longer participate in the festival.{{Cite book|author=Takashi Sawa, et al. |title=Kyoto Encyclopedia |publisher=Tankosha |year=1984 |page=135 |isbn=4-473-00885-1}}{{Cite book|author=Itsuo Munemasa, Katsuhisa Moriya |title=Kyoto Saijiki |publisher=Tankosha |year=1986 |page=262 |isbn=4-473-00940-8}} * {{ill|November 16|en|November 16|WD=Q3029|lt=November 16}} - Fire Ritual Festival (Hitaki-sai) * {{ill|November 23|en|November 23|WD=Q3021|lt=November 23}} - Niiname-sai (Harvest Festival) * {{ill|December 2|en|December 2|WD=Q2296|lt=December 2}} - Ame-no-Yashiro Festival * {{ill|December 31|en|December 31|WD=Q2912|lt=December 31}} - Toshikoshi-no-Harae (Year-End Purification) == Enshrined Deities== * Main Deities - [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Kushinadahime]], [[Gonansanjoshin|Three Female and Five Male Deities]] == Shrine Grounds== * Main Hall * Worship Hall * Ame-no-Yashiro - Deities enshrined: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto]], Kukuno-no-Mikoto, [[Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto]], {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto}}, [[Mizuhanome|Mizuhanome-no-Mikoto]] * Shrine Office * Miyashige {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari shrine}} - Deities enshrined: [[Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ebisu|Hiruko-no-Ōkami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Ōkami]] File:Main shrine of Okazaki-jinja.jpg|Many visitors come during the Year of the Rabbit (2023) File:岡崎神社 - 入口石鳥居.jpg|Stone torii at the entrance File:岡崎神社 - 手水舎.JPG|Chozuya (water purification basin) File:岡崎神社 - 宮繁稲荷神社.JPG|Miyashige Inari Shrine File:岡崎神社 - 狛うさぎ1.JPG|Koma-usagi (guardian rabbits) File:Rabbit statue of Okazaki-jinja 001.jpg|Child-granting rabbit statue == Access== === Bus Routes=== * [[Kyoto City Bus]] ''' "Okazaki Shrine-mae" Bus Stop (Routes 32, 93, 203, 204) {{Cite web|url=https://www2.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kotsu/busdia/hyperdia/menu463.htm |title=Okazaki Shrine-mae |publisher=Kyoto City Office |accessdate=2023-03-22}} === Nearest Train Stations=== * [[Kyoto Municipal Subway Tōzai Line]] - {{ill|Keage Station|en|Keage Station|WD=Q1004370|lt=Keage Station}} * {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|en|Keihan Electric Railway|WD=Q1188274|lt=Keihan Electric Railway}} - {{ill|Jingū-Marutamachi Station|en|Jingū-Marutamachi Station|WD=Q2244767|lt=Jingū-Marutamachi Station}} {{Shinto navigation}} [[snOkazaki Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|岡崎神社|version=98033517|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Okazaki-jinja}} [[ja:岡崎神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11472971}} [[Category:Buildings in Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:History of Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Okazaki, Sakyō-ku]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:t, [[Taira no Tokuko]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

''Showa News Encyclopedia, Vol. 4 (Showa 8–9)''. == Annual Festivals== A signboard at the entrance lists the annual festivals as follows: * {{ill|January 1|en|January 1|WD=Q2150|lt=January 1|1=January 1}} - New Year's Festival (Saitan-sai) * {{ill|January 16|en|January 16|WD=Q2261|lt=January 16|1=January 16}} - Miyashige Inari Festival / Senbo Festival * {{ill|February 3|en|February 3|WD=Q2315|lt=February 3|1=February 3}} - Setsubun Festival * {{ill|March 16|en|March 16|WD=Q2417|lt=March 16|1=March 16}} - Kinensai (Prayer Festival) * {{ill|March|en|March|WD=Q110|lt=March|1=March}} (Spring Equinox) - Spring Ancestral Festival * {{ill|May 16|en|May 16|WD=Q2574|lt=May 16|1=May 16}} - Senbo Festival * {{ill|June 30|en|June 30|WD=Q2657|lt=June 30|1=June 30}} - Nagoshi-no-Harae (Summer Purification) * {{ill|July 16|en|July 16|WD=Q2731|lt=July 16|1=July 16}} - Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * {{ill|September 16|en|September 16|WD=Q2897|lt=September 16|1=September 16}} - Senbo Festival * {{ill|September|en|September|WD=Q123|lt=September|1=September}} (Autumn Equinox) - Autumn Ancestral Festival * {{ill|October 16|en|October 16|WD=Q2963|lt=October 16|1=October 16}} - Parishioners' Festival ** Includes a children's procession, children's portable shrine (mikoshi), and hot-water purification ritual (Yugama). Also known as the Okazaki Tenno Festival, Okazaki Festival, or Grand Festival. Historically, there were more than ten large decorative floats (hoko), but they no longer participate in the festival.{{Cite book|author=Takashi Sawa, et al. |title=Kyoto Encyclopedia |publisher=Tankosha |year=1984 |page=135 |isbn=4-473-00885-1}}{{Cite book|author=Itsuo Munemasa, Katsuhisa Moriya |title=Kyoto Saijiki |publisher=Tankosha |year=1986 |page=262 |isbn=4-473-00940-8}} * {{ill|November 16|en|November 16|WD=Q3029|lt=November 16|1=November 16}} - Ame-no-Yashiro Festival * {{ill|December 31|en|December 31|WD=Q2912|lt=December 31|1=December 31}} - Toshikoshi-no-Harae (Year-End Purification) == Enshrined Deities== * Main Deities - [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Kushinadahime]], [[Gonansanjoshin|Three Female and Five Male Deities]] == Shrine Grounds== * Main Hall * Worship Hall * Ame-no-Yashiro - [[co:commons:Category:Okazaki-jinja]][[ja:岡崎神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Okazaki-jinja]][[ja:岡崎神社]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Okazaki-jinja]][[ja:岡崎神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Okazaki-jinja haiden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Sakyō-ku|WD=Q1321832|1=Sakyō-ku, Kyoto}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.017417|N|135.788778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{otheruses|Shinto shrine in Kyoto, Japan|Shinto shrine in Hiroshima, Japan|Okazaki Shrine (Hiroshima)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |Name = Okazaki Shrine |image = [[File:Okazaki-jinja haiden.jpg|260px]]
Haiden |Location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]], [[Sakyo Ward]], [[Okazaki (Kyoto)|Okazaki]] Higashitenno-cho 51
{{Cite web|url=http://www.okazakijinja.jp/access/|title=Access|publisher=Okazaki Shrine|accessdate=2017-01-25|ref=}} |Coordinates={{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point|frame-width=200}} |Deity = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]]
[[Gonansanjoshin|Three Female and Five Male Deities]] |Shintai = |Shrine Rank = Former [[Gōsha|District Shrine]] |Established = {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} 13 (794 AD) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Other names = Higashitenno-sha, Higashitenno |Pilgrimage = [[Kyoto Jurokushajin]]
[[Zenkoku Seiseikai]] (59 Shrines Enshrining Susanoo-no-Mikoto) |Annual festival = {{ill|July 16|en|July 16|WD=Q2731|lt=July 16|1=July 16}} (Summer Purification Festival) |Rituals = |Map = {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point|frame-width=200}} }} {{osm box|w|1155362848}} '''Okazaki Shrine''' (岡崎神社, Okazaki Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Okazaki (Kyoto)|Okazaki Higashitenno-cho]], [[Sakyo Ward]], [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]]. It is also known as "Higashitenno." Since the surrounding area was once inhabited by [[:en:wild rabbits|wild rabbits]],{{Cite news|title=Full of rabbits – overcoming hardships with leaps and bounds |newspaper=Asahi Shimbun |page=31 |date=2023-01-16 |author=}} rabbits are considered the shrine’s [[Ujigami|guardian spirits]] and {{ill|Shinshi|en|Shinshi|WD=Q10444973|lt=divine messengers|1=Shinshi}}. == Overview== Besides being known as a deity of safe childbirth, the shrine is also believed to grant fertility, as rabbits are known for their prolific reproduction. Additionally, its enshrined deities, [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Kushinadahime]], are associated with having many children. The shrine is also regarded as a place of [[katatagae|directional protection]] due to its historical origins. Several rabbit stone statues can be found throughout the precincts. == History== In {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} 13 (794 AD), during Emperor [[:en:Kanmu|Kanmu]]’s relocation of the capital to {{ill|Heian-kyō|en|Heian-kyō|WD=Q751907|lt=Heian-kyō|1=Heian-kyō}}, the shrine was established as one of the [[Taishogun-sha]] (Great General Shrines) built at the four corners of the city to protect the capital. Since it was located to the east, it became known as "Higashitenno."{{Cite book|author= |title=Kyoto Encyclopedia |publisher=Naoya Kaji |date=1984-11-12 |page=135 |isbn=4-473-00885-1}} In {{ill|Jogan|en|Jogan|WD=Q6214690|lt=Jogan|1=Jogan}} 11 (869 AD), Emperor {{ill|Seiwa|en|Seiwa|WD=Q16871613|lt=Seiwa|1=Seiwa}} ordered the construction of the shrine, enshrining [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]] (identified with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]) from [[Hiromine Shrine]] in {{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|WD=Q1047523|lt=Harima Province|1=Harima Province}}. At the time, it served as the guardian shrine of [[Tokoji Temple]], which was located in the same area. In {{ill|Jisho|en|Jisho|WD=Q114978004|lt=Jisho|1=Jisho}} 2 (1178 AD), Emperor {{ill|Takakura|en|Takakura|WD=Q7677112|lt=Takakura|1=Takakura}} dedicated offerings for the safe childbirth of his co]]. In {{ill|Gen'ō|en|Gen'ō|WD=Q1193925|lt=Gen'ō|1=Gen'ō}} 1 (1319 AD), Emperor [[:en:Go-Daigo|Go-Daigo]] rebuilt the shrine and granted it the Rank of Shoichii (Senior First Rank) along with sacred treasures. During the {{ill|Ōnin War|en|Ōnin War|WD=Q385676|lt=Ōnin War|1=Ōnin War}}, the temple was destroyed by fire, leaving only the shrine intact.{{Cite book|title=Showa Kyoto Guidebook |publisher=Obuchi Kaoru |date=1981-10-30 |page=179}} During the [[:en:Keichō era|Keichō era]] (1596–1615), the shrine was renamed to its current name. In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, it was designated as a [[Gōsha|District Shrine]]. On [[September 21, 1934]], the [[:en:Muroto Typhoon|Muroto Typhoon]] caused strong winds that collapsed the Haiden (worship hall).Many were trapped under collapsed school buildings in Kyoto. ''Osaka Mainichi Shimbun'' (September 21, 1934). ''Showa News Encyclopedia, Vol. 4 (Showa 8–9)''. == Annual Festivals== A signboard at the entrance lists the annual festivals as follows: * {{ill|January 1|en|January 1|WD=Q2150|lt=January 1|1=January 1}} - New Year's Festival (Saitan-sai) * {{ill|January 16|en|January 16|WD=Q2261|lt=January 16|1=January 16}} - Miyashige Inari Festival / Senbo Festival * {{ill|February 3|en|February 3|WD=Q2315|lt=February 3|1=February 3}} - Setsubun Festival * {{ill|March 16|en|March 16|WD=Q2417|lt=March 16|1=March 16}} - Kinensai (Prayer Festival) * {{ill|March|en|March|WD=Q110|lt=March|1=March}} (Spring Equinox) - Spring Ancestral Festival * {{ill|May 16|en|May 16|WD=Q2574|lt=May 16|1=May 16}} - Senbo Festival * {{ill|June 30|en|June 30|WD=Q2657|lt=June 30|1=June 30}} - Nagoshi-no-Harae (Summer Purification) * {{ill|July 16|en|July 16|WD=Q2731|lt=July 16|1=July 16}} - Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * {{ill|September 16|en|September 16|WD=Q2897|lt=September 16|1=September 16}} - Senbo Festival * {{ill|September|en|September|WD=Q123|lt=September|1=September}} (Autumn Equinox) - Autumn Ancestral Festival * {{ill|October 16|en|October 16|WD=Q2963|lt=October 16|1=October 16}} - Parishioners' Festival ** Includes a children's procession, children's portable shrine (mikoshi), and hot-water purification ritual (Yugama). Also known as the Okazaki Tenno Festival, Okazaki Festival, or Grand Festival. Historically, there were more than ten large decorative floats (hoko), but they no longer participate in the festival.{{Cite book|author=Takashi Sawa, et al. |title=Kyoto Encyclopedia |publisher=Tankosha |year=1984 |page=135 |isbn=4-473-00885-1}}{{Cite book|author=Itsuo Munemasa, Katsuhisa Moriya |title=Kyoto Saijiki |publisher=Tankosha |year=1986 |page=262 |isbn=4-473-00940-8}} * {{ill|November 16|en|November 16|WD=Q3029|lt=November 16|1=November 16}} - Fire Ritual Festival (Hitaki-sai) * {{ill|November 23|en|November 23|WD=Q3021|lt=November 23|1=November 23}} - Niiname-sai (Harvest Festival) * {{ill|December 2|en|December 2|WD=Q2296|lt=December 2|1=December 2}} - Deities enshrined: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto]], Kukuno-no-Mikoto, [[Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto]], {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}}, [[Mizuhanome|Mizuhanome-no-Mikoto]] * Shrine Office * Miyashige {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari shrine|1=Inari shrine}} - Deities enshrined: [[Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ebisu|Hiruko-no-Ōkami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Ōkami]] File:Main shrine of Okazaki-jinja.jpg|Many visitors come during the Year of the Rabbit (2023) File:岡崎神社 - 入口石鳥居.jpg|Stone torii at the entrance File:岡崎神社 - 手水舎.JPG|Chozuya (water purification basin) File:岡崎神社 - 宮繁稲荷神社.JPG|Miyashige Inari Shrine File:岡崎神社 - 狛うさぎ1.JPG|Koma-usagi (guardian rabbits) File:Rabbit statue of Okazaki-jinja 001.jpg|Child-granting rabbit statue == Access== === Bus Routes=== * [[Kyoto City Bus]] ''' "Okazaki Shrine-mae" Bus Stop (Routes 32, 93, 203, 204) {{Cite web|url=https://www2.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kotsu/busdia/hyperdia/menu463.htm |title=Okazaki Shrine-mae |publisher=Kyoto City Office |accessdate=2023-03-22}} === Nearest Train Stations=== * [[Kyoto Municipal Subway Tōzai Line]] - {{ill|Keage Station|en|Keage Station|WD=Q1004370|lt=Keage Station|1=Keage Station}} * {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|en|Keihan Electric Railway|WD=Q1188274|lt=Keihan Electric Railway|1=Keihan Electric Railway}} - {{ill|Jingū-Marutamachi Station|en|Jingū-Marutamachi Station|WD=Q2244767|lt=Jingū-Marutamachi Station|1=Jingū-Marutamachi Station}} {{Shinto navigation}} [[snOkazaki Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|岡崎神社|version=98033517|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Okazaki-jinja}} [[ja:岡崎神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11472971}} [[Category:Buildings in Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:History of Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Okazaki, Sakyō-ku]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:t, [[Taira no Tokuko]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Okazaki-jinja]][[ja:岡崎神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Okazaki-jinja]][[ja:岡崎神社]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Okazaki-jinja]][[ja:岡崎神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Okazaki-jinja haiden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Sakyō-ku|WD=Q1321832|1=Sakyō-ku, Kyoto}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.017417|N|135.788778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{otheruses|Shinto shrine in Kyoto, Japan|Shinto shrine in Hiroshima, Japan|Okazaki Shrine (Hiroshima)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |Name = Okazaki Shrine |image = [[File:Okazaki-jinja haiden.jpg|260px]]
Haiden |Location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]], [[Sakyo Ward]], [[Okazaki (Kyoto)|Okazaki]] Higashitenno-cho 51
{{Cite web|url=http://www.okazakijinja.jp/access/|title=Access|publisher=Okazaki Shrine|accessdate=2017-01-25|ref=}} |Coordinates={{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point|frame-width=200}} |Deity = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]]
[[Gonansanjoshin|Three Female and Five Male Deities]] |Shintai = |Shrine Rank = Former [[Gōsha|District Shrine]] |Established = {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} 13 (794 AD) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Other names = Higashitenno-sha, Higashitenno |Pilgrimage = [[Kyoto Jurokushajin]]
[[Zenkoku Seiseikai]] (59 Shrines Enshrining Susanoo-no-Mikoto) |Annual festival = {{ill|July 16|en|July 16|WD=Q2731|lt=July 16|1=July 16}} (Summer Purification Festival) |Rituals = |Map = {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point|frame-width=200}} }} {{osm box|w|1155362848}} '''Okazaki Shrine''' (岡崎神社, Okazaki Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Okazaki (Kyoto)|Okazaki Higashitenno-cho]], [[Sakyo Ward]], [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]]. It is also known as "Higashitenno." Since the surrounding area was once inhabited by [[:en:wild rabbits|wild rabbits]],{{Cite news|title=Full of rabbits – overcoming hardships with leaps and bounds |newspaper=Asahi Shimbun |page=31 |date=2023-01-16 |author=}} rabbits are considered the shrine’s [[Ujigami|guardian spirits]] and {{ill|Shinshi|en|Shinshi|WD=Q10444973|lt=divine messengers|1=Shinshi}}. == Overview== Besides being known as a deity of safe childbirth, the shrine is also believed to grant fertility, as rabbits are known for their prolific reproduction. Additionally, its enshrined deities, [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Kushinadahime]], are associated with having many children. The shrine is also regarded as a place of [[katatagae|directional protection]] due to its historical origins. Several rabbit stone statues can be found throughout the precincts. == History== In {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} 13 (794 AD), during Emperor [[:en:Kanmu|Kanmu]]’s relocation of the capital to {{ill|Heian-kyō|en|Heian-kyō|WD=Q751907|lt=Heian-kyō|1=Heian-kyō}}, the shrine was established as one of the [[Taishogun-sha]] (Great General Shrines) built at the four corners of the city to protect the capital. Since it was located to the east, it became known as "Higashitenno."{{Cite book|author= |title=Kyoto Encyclopedia |publisher=Naoya Kaji |date=1984-11-12 |page=135 |isbn=4-473-00885-1}} In {{ill|Jogan|en|Jogan|WD=Q6214690|lt=Jogan|1=Jogan}} 11 (869 AD), Emperor {{ill|Seiwa|en|Seiwa|WD=Q16871613|lt=Seiwa|1=Seiwa}} ordered the construction of the shrine, enshrining [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]] (identified with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]) from [[Hiromine Shrine]] in {{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|WD=Q1047523|lt=Harima Province|1=Harima Province}}. At the time, it served as the guardian shrine of [[Tokoji Temple]], which was located in the same area. In {{ill|Jisho|en|Jisho|WD=Q114978004|lt=Jisho|1=Jisho}} 2 (1178 AD), Emperor {{ill|Takakura|en|Takakura|WD=Q7677112|lt=Takakura|1=Takakura}} dedicated offerings for the safe childbirth of his co]]. In {{ill|Gen'ō|en|Gen'ō|WD=Q1193925|lt=Gen'ō|1=Gen'ō}} 1 (1319 AD), Emperor [[:en:Go-Daigo|Go-Daigo]] rebuilt the shrine and granted it the Rank of Shoichii (Senior First Rank) along with sacred treasures. During the {{ill|Ōnin War|en|Ōnin War|WD=Q385676|lt=Ōnin War|1=Ōnin War}}, the temple was destroyed by fire, leaving only the shrine intact.{{Cite book|title=Showa Kyoto Guidebook |publisher=Obuchi Kaoru |date=1981-10-30 |page=179}} During the [[:en:Keichō era|Keichō era]] (1596–1615), the shrine was renamed to its current name. In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, it was designated as a [[Gōsha|District Shrine]]. On [[September 21, 1934]], the [[:en:Muroto Typhoon|Muroto Typhoon]] caused strong winds that collapsed the Haiden (worship hall).Many were trapped under collapsed school buildings in Kyoto. ''Osaka Mainichi Shimbun'' (September 21, 1934). ''Showa News Encyclopedia, Vol. 4 (Showa 8–9)''. == Annual Festivals== A signboard at the entrance lists the annual festivals as follows: * {{ill|January 1|en|January 1|WD=Q2150|lt=January 1|1=January 1}} - New Year's Festival (Saitan-sai) * {{ill|January 16|en|January 16|WD=Q2261|lt=January 16|1=January 16}} - Miyashige Inari Festival / Senbo Festival * {{ill|February 3|en|February 3|WD=Q2315|lt=February 3|1=February 3}} - Setsubun Festival * {{ill|March 16|en|March 16|WD=Q2417|lt=March 16|1=March 16}} - Kinensai (Prayer Festival) * {{ill|March|en|March|WD=Q110|lt=March|1=March}} (Spring Equinox) - Spring Ancestral Festival * {{ill|May 16|en|May 16|WD=Q2574|lt=May 16|1=May 16}} - Senbo Festival * {{ill|June 30|en|June 30|WD=Q2657|lt=June 30|1=June 30}} - Nagoshi-no-Harae (Summer Purification) * {{ill|July 16|en|July 16|WD=Q2731|lt=July 16|1=July 16}} - Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * {{ill|September 16|en|September 16|WD=Q2897|lt=September 16|1=September 16}} - Senbo Festival * {{ill|September|en|September|WD=Q123|lt=September|1=September}} (Autumn Equinox) - Autumn Ancestral Festival * {{ill|October 16|en|October 16|WD=Q2963|lt=October 16|1=October 16}} - Parishioners' Festival ** Includes a children's procession, children's portable shrine (mikoshi), and hot-water purification ritual (Yugama). Also known as the Okazaki Tenno Festival, Okazaki Festival, or Grand Festival. Historically, there were more than ten large decorative floats (hoko), but they no longer participate in the festival.{{Cite book|author=Takashi Sawa, et al. |title=Kyoto Encyclopedia |publisher=Tankosha |year=1984 |page=135 |isbn=4-473-00885-1}}{{Cite book|author=Itsuo Munemasa, Katsuhisa Moriya |title=Kyoto Saijiki |publisher=Tankosha |year=1986 |page=262 |isbn=4-473-00940-8}} * {{ill|November 16|en|November 16|WD=Q3029|lt=November 16|1=November 16}} - Fire Ritual Festival (Hitaki-sai) * {{ill|November 23|en|November 23|WD=Q3021|lt=November 23|1=November 23}} - Niiname-sai (Harvest Festival) * {{ill|December 2|en|December 2|WD=Q2296|lt=December 2|1=December 2}} - Ame-no-Yashiro Festival * {{ill|December 31|en|December 31|WD=Q2912|lt=December 31|1=December 31}} - Toshikoshi-no-Harae (Year-End Purification) == Enshrined Deities== * Main Deities - [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Kushinadahime]], [[Gonansanjoshin|Three Female and Five Male Deities]] == Shrine Grounds== * Main Hall * Worship Hall * Ame-no-Yashiro - Deities enshrined: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto]], Kukuno-no-Mikoto, [[Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto]], {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}}, [[Mizuhanome|Mizuhanome-no-Mikoto]] * Shrine Office * Miyashige {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari shrine|1=Inari shrine}} - Deities enshrined: [[Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ebisu|Hiruko-no-Ōkami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Ōkami]] File:Main shrine of Okazaki-jinja.jpg|Many visitors come during the Year of the Rabbit (2023) File:岡崎神社 - 入口石鳥居.jpg|Stone torii at the entrance File:岡崎神社 - 手水舎.JPG|Chozuya (water purification basin) File:岡崎神社 - 宮繁稲荷神社.JPG|Miyashige Inari Shrine File:岡崎神社 - 狛うさぎ1.JPG|Koma-usagi (guardian rabbits) File:Rabbit statue of Okazaki-jinja 001.jpg|Child-granting rabbit statue == Access== === Bus Routes=== * [[Kyoto City Bus]] ''' "Okazaki Shrine-mae" Bus Stop (Routes 32, 93, 203, 204) {{Cite web|url=https://www2.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kotsu/busdia/hyperdia/menu463.htm |title=Okazaki Shrine-mae |publisher=Kyoto City Office |accessdate=2023-03-22}} === Nearest Train Stations=== * [[Kyoto Municipal Subway Tōzai Line]] - {{ill|Keage Station|en|Keage Station|WD=Q1004370|lt=Keage Station|1=Keage Station}} * {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|en|Keihan Electric Railway|WD=Q1188274|lt=Keihan Electric Railway|1=Keihan Electric Railway}} - {{ill|Jingū-Marutamachi Station|en|Jingū-Marutamachi Station|WD=Q2244767|lt=Jingū-Marutamachi Station|1=Jingū-Marutamachi Station}} {{Shinto navigation}} [[snOkazaki Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|岡崎神社|version=98033517|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Okazaki-jinja}} [[ja:岡崎神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11472971}} [[Category:Buildings in Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:History of Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Okazaki, Sakyō-ku]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:t, [[Taira no Tokuko]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Okazaki-jinja]][[ja:岡崎神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Okazaki-jinja]][[ja:岡崎神社]] [[ja:岡崎神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Okazaki-jinja haiden.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Sakyō-ku|WD=Q1321832|1=Sakyō-ku, Kyoto}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.017417|N|135.788778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{otheruses|Shinto shrine in Kyoto, Japan|Shinto shrine in Hiroshima, Japan|Okazaki Shrine (Hiroshima)}} {{infobox Shinto shrine |Name = Okazaki Shrine |image = [[File:Okazaki-jinja haiden.jpg|260px]]
Haiden |Location = [[Kyoto Prefecture]], [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]], [[Sakyo Ward]], [[Okazaki (Kyoto)|Okazaki]] Higashitenno-cho 51
{{Cite web|url=http://www.okazakijinja.jp/access/|title=Access|publisher=Okazaki Shrine|accessdate=2017-01-25|ref=}} |Coordinates={{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point|frame-width=200}} |Deity = [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]]
[[Kushinadahime|Kushinadahime-no-Mikoto]]
[[Gonansanjoshin|Three Female and Five Male Deities]] |Shintai = |Shrine Rank = Former [[Gōsha|District Shrine]] |Established = {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} 13 (794 AD) |Chief priest = |Main hall = |Other names = Higashitenno-sha, Higashitenno |Pilgrimage = [[Kyoto Jurokushajin]]
[[Zenkoku Seiseikai]] (59 Shrines Enshrining Susanoo-no-Mikoto) |Annual festival = {{ill|July 16|en|July 16|WD=Q2731|lt=July 16|1=July 16}} (Summer Purification Festival) |Rituals = |Map = {{Infobox mapframe|zoom=12|type=point|frame-width=200}} }} {{osm box|w|1155362848}} '''Okazaki Shrine''' (岡崎神社, Okazaki Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Okazaki (Kyoto)|Okazaki Higashitenno-cho]], [[Sakyo Ward]], [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]]. It is also known as "Higashitenno." Since the surrounding area was once inhabited by [[:en:wild rabbits|wild rabbits]],{{Cite news|title=Full of rabbits – overcoming hardships with leaps and bounds |newspaper=Asahi Shimbun |page=31 |date=2023-01-16 |author=}} rabbits are considered the shrine’s [[Ujigami|guardian spirits]] and {{ill|Shinshi|en|Shinshi|WD=Q10444973|lt=divine messengers|1=Shinshi}}. == Overview== Besides being known as a deity of safe childbirth, the shrine is also believed to grant fertility, as rabbits are known for their prolific reproduction. Additionally, its enshrined deities, [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] and [[Kushinadahime]], are associated with having many children. The shrine is also regarded as a place of [[katatagae|directional protection]] due to its historical origins. Several rabbit stone statues can be found throughout the precincts. == History== In {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|WD=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} 13 (794 AD), during Emperor [[:en:Kanmu|Kanmu]]’s relocation of the capital to {{ill|Heian-kyō|en|Heian-kyō|WD=Q751907|lt=Heian-kyō|1=Heian-kyō}}, the shrine was established as one of the [[Taishogun-sha]] (Great General Shrines) built at the four corners of the city to protect the capital. Since it was located to the east, it became known as "Higashitenno."{{Cite book|author= |title=Kyoto Encyclopedia |publisher=Naoya Kaji |date=1984-11-12 |page=135 |isbn=4-473-00885-1}} In {{ill|Jogan|en|Jogan|WD=Q6214690|lt=Jogan|1=Jogan}} 11 (869 AD), Emperor {{ill|Seiwa|en|Seiwa|WD=Q16871613|lt=Seiwa|1=Seiwa}} ordered the construction of the shrine, enshrining [[Gozu Tennō|Gozu Tenno]] (identified with [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]]) from [[Hiromine Shrine]] in {{ill|Harima Province|en|Harima Province|WD=Q1047523|lt=Harima Province|1=Harima Province}}. At the time, it served as the guardian shrine of [[Tokoji Temple]], which was located in the same area. In {{ill|Jisho|en|Jisho|WD=Q114978004|lt=Jisho|1=Jisho}} 2 (1178 AD), Emperor {{ill|Takakura|en|Takakura|WD=Q7677112|lt=Takakura|1=Takakura}} dedicated offerings for the safe childbirth of his co]]. In {{ill|Gen'ō|en|Gen'ō|WD=Q1193925|lt=Gen'ō|1=Gen'ō}} 1 (1319 AD), Emperor [[:en:Go-Daigo|Go-Daigo]] rebuilt the shrine and granted it the Rank of Shoichii (Senior First Rank) along with sacred treasures. During the {{ill|Ōnin War|en|Ōnin War|WD=Q385676|lt=Ōnin War|1=Ōnin War}}, the temple was destroyed by fire, leaving only the shrine intact.{{Cite book|title=Showa Kyoto Guidebook |publisher=Obuchi Kaoru |date=1981-10-30 |page=179}} During the [[:en:Keichō era|Keichō era]] (1596–1615), the shrine was renamed to its current name. In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, it was designated as a [[Gōsha|District Shrine]]. On [[September 21, 1934]], the [[:en:Muroto Typhoon|Muroto Typhoon]] caused strong winds that collapsed the Haiden (worship hall).Many were trapped under collapsed school buildings in Kyoto. ''Osaka Mainichi Shimbun'' (September 21, 1934). ''Showa News Encyclopedia, Vol. 4 (Showa 8–9)''. == Annual Festivals== A signboard at the entrance lists the annual festivals as follows: * {{ill|January 1|en|January 1|WD=Q2150|lt=January 1|1=January 1}} - New Year's Festival (Saitan-sai) * {{ill|January 16|en|January 16|WD=Q2261|lt=January 16|1=January 16}} - Miyashige Inari Festival / Senbo Festival * {{ill|February 3|en|February 3|WD=Q2315|lt=February 3|1=February 3}} - Setsubun Festival * {{ill|March 16|en|March 16|WD=Q2417|lt=March 16|1=March 16}} - Kinensai (Prayer Festival) * {{ill|March|en|March|WD=Q110|lt=March|1=March}} (Spring Equinox) - Spring Ancestral Festival * {{ill|May 16|en|May 16|WD=Q2574|lt=May 16|1=May 16}} - Senbo Festival * {{ill|June 30|en|June 30|WD=Q2657|lt=June 30|1=June 30}} - Nagoshi-no-Harae (Summer Purification) * {{ill|July 16|en|July 16|WD=Q2731|lt=July 16|1=July 16}} - Grand Festival (Reitaisai) * {{ill|September 16|en|September 16|WD=Q2897|lt=September 16|1=September 16}} - Senbo Festival * {{ill|September|en|September|WD=Q123|lt=September|1=September}} (Autumn Equinox) - Autumn Ancestral Festival * {{ill|October 16|en|October 16|WD=Q2963|lt=October 16|1=October 16}} - Parishioners' Festival ** Includes a children's procession, children's portable shrine (mikoshi), and hot-water purification ritual (Yugama). Also known as the Okazaki Tenno Festival, Okazaki Festival, or Grand Festival. Historically, there were more than ten large decorative floats (hoko), but they no longer participate in the festival.{{Cite book|author=Takashi Sawa, et al. |title=Kyoto Encyclopedia |publisher=Tankosha |year=1984 |page=135 |isbn=4-473-00885-1}}{{Cite book|author=Itsuo Munemasa, Katsuhisa Moriya |title=Kyoto Saijiki |publisher=Tankosha |year=1986 |page=262 |isbn=4-473-00940-8}} * {{ill|November 16|en|November 16|WD=Q3029|lt=November 16|1=November 16}} - Fire Ritual Festival (Hitaki-sai) * {{ill|November 23|en|November 23|WD=Q3021|lt=November 23|1=November 23}} - Niiname-sai (Harvest Festival) * {{ill|December 2|en|December 2|WD=Q2296|lt=December 2|1=December 2}} - Ame-no-Yashiro Festival * {{ill|December 31|en|December 31|WD=Q2912|lt=December 31|1=December 31}} - Toshikoshi-no-Harae (Year-End Purification) == Enshrined Deities== * Main Deities - [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Hayatano Susanoo-no-Mikoto]], [[Kushinadahime]], [[Gonansanjoshin|Three Female and Five Male Deities]] == Shrine Grounds== * Main Hall * Worship Hall * Ame-no-Yashiro - Deities enshrined: [[Ōyamatsumi|Ōyamatsumi-no-Mikoto]], Kukuno-no-Mikoto, [[Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto|Kunisatsuchi-no-Mikoto]], {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Mikoto|1=Toyouke-hime}}, [[Mizuhanome|Mizuhanome-no-Mikoto]] * Shrine Office * Miyashige {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari shrine|1=Inari shrine}} - Deities enshrined: [[Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Ebisu|Hiruko-no-Ōkami]], [[Ōkuninushi|Ōkuninushi-no-Ōkami]] File:Main shrine of Okazaki-jinja.jpg|Many visitors come during the Year of the Rabbit (2023) File:岡崎神社 - 入口石鳥居.jpg|Stone torii at the entrance File:岡崎神社 - 手水舎.JPG|Chozuya (water purification basin) File:岡崎神社 - 宮繁稲荷神社.JPG|Miyashige Inari Shrine File:岡崎神社 - 狛うさぎ1.JPG|Koma-usagi (guardian rabbits) File:Rabbit statue of Okazaki-jinja 001.jpg|Child-granting rabbit statue == Access== === Bus Routes=== * [[Kyoto City Bus]] ''' "Okazaki Shrine-mae" Bus Stop (Routes 32, 93, 203, 204) {{Cite web|url=https://www2.city.kyoto.lg.jp/kotsu/busdia/hyperdia/menu463.htm |title=Okazaki Shrine-mae |publisher=Kyoto City Office |accessdate=2023-03-22}} === Nearest Train Stations=== * [[Kyoto Municipal Subway Tōzai Line]] - {{ill|Keage Station|en|Keage Station|WD=Q1004370|lt=Keage Station|1=Keage Station}} * {{ill|Keihan Electric Railway|en|Keihan Electric Railway|WD=Q1188274|lt=Keihan Electric Railway|1=Keihan Electric Railway}} - {{ill|Jingū-Marutamachi Station|en|Jingū-Marutamachi Station|WD=Q2244767|lt=Jingū-Marutamachi Station|1=Jingū-Marutamachi Station}} {{Shinto navigation}} [[snOkazaki Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|岡崎神社|version=98033517|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Okazaki-jinja}} [[ja:岡崎神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11472971}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Gion shrines]] [[Category:History of Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Okazaki, Sakyō-ku]] [[Category:Onmyōdō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:t, [[Taira no Tokuko]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

''Osaka Mainichi Shimbun'' (September 21, 1934).
